items.title: Broadcom and OpenAI unveil custom-built Jalapeño inference processor — OpenAI's first chip is a massive reticle-sized ASIC built in an ultra-fast nine-month development cycle
items.description: Broadcom and OpenAI reveal their Jalapeño custom-built inference ASIC that allegedly beats existing leading-edge in terms of performance-per-watt.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter OpenAI and Broadcom have introduced Jalapeño, a custom-built inference processor designed specifically for modern large language models and future agentic AI workloads, which is designed to deliver performance per watt they claim is higher than today's leading-edge hardware. OpenAI considers its hardware project a strategic one and envisions Jalapeño to be the first generation of its inference hardware.Not another AI acceleratorOpenAI stresses that Jalapeño is a purpose-built inference ASIC and not a repurposed training accelerator or a general-purpose AI processor. OpenAI says the architecture of Jalapeño was designed based on its understanding of LLM behavior and is meant to address practical bottlenecks that matter for inference at scale, including costly data movement, balance between compute and memory resources, networking efficiency, and overall behavior. OpenAI also states that the design of the processor is meant to wed high throughput with low latency (which is why it uses a huge compute chiplet and HBM memory and not cheaper types of DRAM like many other inference accelerators), which will be particularly handy for reasoning and agentic workloads.In addition, OpenAI and Broadcom claim the processor is built to deliver higher effective utilization than conventional AI accelerators and deliver performance that is close to the theoretical maximum, which means very high efficiency both in terms of costs and in terms of power. Meanwhile, the companies did not disclose performance targets for their Jalapeño ASIC, so these claims should be taken with a grain of salt.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Engineering samples are already operating in the lab at target clock speed and power (though Broadcom and OpenAI do not disclose details about this, either), and OpenAI says it is running machine learning workloads, such as GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark.The two companies also claim that early internal testing indicates that Jalapeño's performance-per-watt is substantially better than 'current state-of-the-art hardware,' although no hard numbers, benchmarks, memory configuration, or other details are disclosed, so again, we will have to take the claims with a grain of salt. In addition, one must bear in mind that while Jalapeño can purportedly beat existing AMD's Instinct MI350-series and Nvidia's Blackwell-based accelerators, it remains to be seen how competitive it will be against AMD's Instinct MI400-series and Nvidia's Rubin-based offerings. You may like The custom AI ASIC state of play (May 2026) — Broadcom deals, Google TPUs, Meta MTIA & beyond Inside Google's TPU V8 strategy, delivering two chips for two crucial tasks at incredible scale Broadcom to supply Meta with custom silicon through 2029 "Jalapeño was designed from the ground up for LLM inference using detailed insights from our close collaboration with OpenAI researchers," said Richard Ho, who leads OpenAI's hardware program. "We optimized the architecture around the kernels, memory movement, networking, and serving patterns that matter most for frontier AI models. Based on early testing, Jalapeño will efficiently execute our most important workloads close to the hardware’s theoretical limits."A massive chip with six HBM modulesWhile Broadcom and OpenAI did not disclose specifications of Jalapeño, they did show its wafer and packaging, so we can do a brief analysis. The package appears to contain one large compute chiplet surrounded by six HBM modules and another chiplet that likely packs input/output interfaces and is surrounded by two structural dummy dies.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: OpenAI)The wafer image does look like a Broadcom-style systolic-array-heavy accelerator, in the sense that it shows a very regular, repeated, columnar floorplan with what looks like replicated compute regions and fixed infrastructure macros. Yet, keep in mind that we are speculating, and the image is not clean enough to say that this is definitely Broadcom's standard TPU-like systolic array template with some perks from OpenAI,From the image alone, it is impossible to tell whether Jalapeño uses a true 2D systolic array, a set of 1D/2D matrix engines, a collection of vector or tensor tiles, or some other inference datapath. All we can say is that the die has a highly repetitive floorplan consistent with several kinds of tiled AI accelerator architectures. (Image credit: OpenAI)What we can tell from the image is the approximate die size of Jalapeño's compute chiplet based on the size of HBM3/4 packages (10.975 mm × 10.975 mm) that surround it. From what we can tell, the chiplet measures 25.46 mm (width) × 33 mm (height), which means that its die size is around 840 mm2, which is very close to the reticle size of EUV lithography systems (858 mm2). Given that the quality of the shot is poor, the die size we estimate cannot be 100% accurate, but we suspect it is close enough. What to read next Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 Meta's multi-billion-dollar Graviton deal highlights intensifying CPU shortages in AI infrastructure Intel and SambaNova team up on heterogenous AI inference platform — different hardware performs different workloads The die size of Jalapeño's compute chiplet implies that it packs quite a lot of compute oomph, though, of course, we cannot make performance estimates based on this metric. Yet, it is safe to say that Jalapeño's compute die is considerably bigger than compute dies of other inference accelerators on the market and more resembles processors for AI training. Speaking of processors for AI training, we increasingly see multi-chiplet designs for these workloads as companies like AMD and Nvidia want to pack as much performance as possible. Meanwhile, the fact that OpenAI and Broadcom chose to go with a large compute chiplet possibly indicates that they wanted to reduce latencies by as much as possible.Designed in nine monthsThe companies say the chip reached tape-out in just nine months and is slated for deployment beginning in late 2026, which represents an extremely fast turnaround time in ASIC design. It is unclear whether Broadcom and OpenAI extensively used artificial intelligence to define and then develop Jalapeño, though the companies admitted that they used OpenAI's models to speed up parts of the chip's design and optimization work. Typically, it takes 1.5 – 2 years to design an ASIC from scratch, so AI can shrink the development cycle. Another means to accelerate the design cycle is Broadcom's extensive reuse of its logic across different custom designs to deliver new chips faster than other companies.It is noteworthy that, according to the announcement, Jalapeño is designed to support not only OpenAI's own workloads but also present and future LLMs across the industry, which potentially lets OpenAI sell its hardware to third parties, assuming that it can get enough supply from Broadcom and TSMC. Meanwhile, the chief executive of Broadcom indicates that Jalapeño will be deployed at gigawatt-scale data centers with Microsoft and other partners starting this year, though it is unclear whether the processor will be used exclusively for OpenAI workloads or will be available for other tenants as well."Our collaboration with OpenAI represents a fundamental commitment to scaling the physical infrastructure required for the next decade of AI," said Hock Tan, President and CEO, Broadcom. "This is just the beginning of a multi-generation roadmap. By co-developing our industry-leading silicon directly with OpenAI, we are enabling the deployment of gigawatt-scale data centers with Microsoft and other partners beginning in 2026." items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 博通与OpenAI联合发布定制Jalapeño推理处理器——OpenAI首款芯片是巨大的掩模版级ASIC，开发周期仅九个月，速度极快
items.descriptionZh:
OpenAI与Broadcom联合推出了名为Jalapeño的定制化推理处理器，这款芯片专为现代大语言模型（LLM）及未来的智能体AI（agentic AI）工作负载而设计。OpenAI强调，Jalapeño并非重新利用的训练加速器或通用AI处理器，而是一款纯粹为推理而生的专用ASIC（专用集成电路）。其架构基于OpenAI对LLM行为的深刻理解，旨在解决大规模推理中的实际瓶颈，包括昂贵的数据移动、计算与内存资源的平衡、网络效率以及整体运行行为。为了实现高吞吐量与低延迟的结合（这对推理和智能体任务尤为关键），Jalapeño采用了一个巨大的计算芯粒并搭配HBM高带宽内存，而非许多其他推理加速器所使用的廉价DRAM。

OpenAI和Broadcom声称，Jalapeño能够实现比传统AI加速器更高的有效利用率，其性能接近理论极限，这意味着在成本和功耗方面都具备极高效率。不过，双方并未披露具体的性能目标，因此这些声明需要谨慎看待。据透露，工程样品已在实验室中以目标时钟频率和功耗运行（具体细节同样未公开），OpenAI表示它正在运行GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark等机器学习负载。早期内部测试显示，Jalapeño的每瓦性能“显著优于当前最先进的硬件”，但未提供具体数字、基准测试或内存配置等细节。此外，虽然Jalapeño据称能击败现有的AMD Instinct MI350系列和Nvidia Blackwell加速器，但它能否与未来的AMD Instinct MI400系列和Nvidia Rubin产品竞争，仍有待观察。

从公开的晶圆和封装图像可以分析，Jalapeño的封装包含一个大型计算芯粒，周围环绕着6个HBM模块（很可能是HBM3或HBM4），以及另一个可能集成输入/输出接口的芯粒，旁边有两个结构性的假晶粒。根据HBM封装尺寸（10.975mm × 10.975mm）推算，该计算芯粒的尺寸约为25.46 mm（宽）× 33 mm（高），面积约840 mm²，非常接近EUV光刻系统的掩模板极限尺寸（858 mm²）。这一尺寸意味着Jalapeño的计算芯粒明显大于市场上其他推理加速器，更接近AI训练处理器的规模。与AMD和Nvidia越来越多采用多芯粒设计以堆叠性能不同，OpenAI和Broadcom选择大型单芯粒，可能主要是为了尽可能降低延迟。

值得注意的是，Jalapeño从设计到流片仅用了9个月，这在ASIC开发中极为迅速。双方承认在芯片设计和优化过程中使用了OpenAI的模型来加速部分工作，这显著缩短了通常需要1.5至2年的开发周期。此外，Broadcom在多个定制设计中对逻辑模块的高度复用也是加快交付的关键。

据公告，Jalapeño不仅支持OpenAI自身的工作负载，还旨在兼容行业当前及未来的LLM，这为OpenAI向第三方销售硬件提供了可能（前提是能从Broadcom和台积电获得足够产能）。Broadcom首席执行官Hock Tan表示，Jalapeño将从今年（2026年）开始部署在微软及其他合作伙伴的千兆瓦级数据中心，具体是仅用于OpenAI工作负载还是也对其他租户开放，尚不明确。Tan强调：“通过与OpenAI共同开发行业领先的硅片，我们正从2026年开始支持千兆瓦级数据中心的部署……这只是一个多代路线图的开始。”

items.title: Figma now has AI motion graphics and shader tools
items.description:
Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives "push their ideas further" and automate tedious tasks with AI. Part of this is a reimagined canvas that's now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials "together in one place."
Notable callouts include coding layers that let you tweak the code of your projects without leaving the Figma Design canvas, and AI-generated motion graphics effects, which allow you to make animations and transition effects by simply describing them to Figma's chatbot interface. You c …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives “push their ideas further” and automate tedious tasks with AI. Part of this is a reimagined canvas that’s now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials “together in one place.”Notable callouts include coding layers that let you tweak the code of your projects without leaving the Figma Design canvas, and AI-generated motion graphics effects, which allow you to make animations and transition effects by simply describing them to Figma’s chatbot interface. You can read more about all the new product updates over on Figma’s website.Otherwise, here’s Figma’s overview of everything that was announced:New creative materials to express ideas● Code layers: Work directly with code on the Figma Design canvas. Clone repositories, generate new directions with Figma’s agent, extract flows into editable design layers, and sync changes back to code.● Motion: Design animations, transitions, and 3D transforms collaboratively, directly from Figma. Prompt to create animations with AI, apply preset styles, or adjust manually on a timeline. Motion is connected to design systems, code-backed, and ready to ship.● Shaders: Prompt to build shader effects and fills, custom visual treatments powered by WebGPU. Effects that weren’t available in Figma before, like dither, pixelate, and various blur types can now be created directly on the canvas.● Figma Weave workflows: Generate consistent and high-quality visuals in Figma with 20 plus integrated Weave tools, turning complex AI workflows into simple tools on the canvas. This is the first step towards a full integration between Figma and Figma Weave, expected later this year.New intelligent tools for teams and agents to collaborate● Agent skills and deeper context: Turn repetitive work into skills that entire teams can use to work more precisely and consistently with Figma’s agent. Bring in more context with third party connectors, web search, and file attachments.● Generative plugins: Extend Figma’s capabilities by building custom, reusable plugins with the agent. Turn prompts into tools that can be tweaked and shared — no developer setup or technical skills required.
items.author: Jess Weatherbed
items.titleZh: Figma 现已推出 AI 动态图形和着色器工具
items.descriptionZh:
在年度Config大会上，Figma发布了多项设计和编码产品更新，旨在帮助创意人员“进一步推动想法”，并借助AI自动化繁琐任务。核心亮点之一是重新设计的画布——据Figma介绍，它已针对全栈开发进行了优化，将团队、AI代理、工具和素材“集中在一个地方”。具体来看，更新涵盖以下两大类别：

### 一、新的创意素材，助力表达想法

1. **编码层（Code layers）**：允许用户在Figma设计画布内直接修改项目代码。用户可以克隆代码仓库、利用Figma的AI代理生成新的方向、将流程提取为可编辑的设计图层，以及将更改同步回代码——无需离开设计环境即可完成开发中的代码调整。
2. **动态效果（Motion）**：直接在Figma中协作设计动画、过渡和3D变换。用户可通过AI提示生成动画、应用预设样式，或在时间轴上手动调整。该功能与设计系统相连、基于代码，并可直接投入生产。
3. **着色器（Shaders）**：通过提示构建着色器效果和填充，利用WebGPU实现自定义视觉处理。此前Figma中无法实现的效果（如抖动、像素化、多种模糊类型）现在可直接在画布上创建。
4. **Figma Weave工作流**：通过20多种集成的Weave工具，在Figma中生成一致且高质量的视觉内容，将复杂的AI工作流转化为画布上的简单工具。这是Figma与Figma Weave全面集成（预计今年晚些时候实现）的第一步。

### 二、面向团队和AI代理的新型智能工具

1. **Agent技能与更深上下文**：将重复性工作转化为技能，整个团队可借助Figma的AI代理更精确、一致地工作。通过第三方连接器、网络搜索和文件附件引入更多上下文。
2. **生成式插件（Generative plugins）**：通过代理构建自定义、可复用的插件来扩展Figma功能。用户可将提示转化为可调整和共享的工具——无需任何开发者环境搭建或技术技能。

此次更新在技术原理上强调“一体化”与“低门槛”：通过将编码、动画、视觉特效及复杂AI工作流深度嵌入设计画布，消除了工具切换的摩擦；AI代理则通过自然语言交互（如聊天界面）将原本需要专业技能的步骤简化为描述性操作。性能方面，虽然没有给出具体数字对比，但“实时同步代码”“提示即生成”等特性显著降低了从设计到开发的时间成本和协作复杂度。应用场景涵盖从UI/UX设计、原型动画、视觉特效制作到团队标准化工作流自动化，尤其适合全栈设计师、产品团队以及希望零代码扩展设计工具能力的非技术成员。更多细节可访问Figma官网查看。

items.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
items.description:
OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday.
Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it's designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI inference, models process a user's request to run an agent like Codex or offer a response from ChatGPT, while AI training involves a model consuming vast amounts of data to inform its responses.
It comes just nine months after OpenAI revealed that it would team up with Br …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: OpenAI has just revealed a new “intelligence processor” chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday.Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it’s designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI inference, models process a user’s request to run an agent like Codex or offer a response from ChatGPT, while AI training involves a model consuming vast amounts of data to inform its responses.It comes just nine months after OpenAI revealed that it would team up with Broadcom to make its own chips, helping reduce the company’s reliance on the GPUs offered by Nvidia that are in limited supply. In an interview with Reuters, Broadcom CEO Hock Tan says it matches the performance of Nvidia’s Blackwell chips and Google’s Tensor processing units. Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon are among the other AI companies that have also launched custom-designed AI chips recently to power their servers for either training or inference, while still trailing Nvidia’s chips on overall performance.OpenAI calls Jalapeño the “first step in a multi-generation compute platform,” which it expects to deploy by the end of 2026. “While OpenAI is still measuring final performance, early testing shows that Jalapeño will deliver performance per watt substantially better than current state-of-the-art,” OpenAI says.
items.author: Emma Roth
items.descriptionZh:
OpenAI近日宣布与博通（Broadcom）合作，共同推出了一款名为“Jalapeño”的新型AI服务器专用芯片，该芯片被定位为“智能处理器”。根据周三的官方公告，Jalapeño是一款专用集成电路（ASIC），专为AI推理任务而设计——即处理用户请求、运行如Codex这样的智能代理或生成ChatGPT响应，而非用于AI训练（训练需要模型消化海量数据以优化回答能力）。这是OpenAI九个月前宣布与博通联手自研芯片后的成果，旨在减少对英伟达（Nvidia）供应紧张的GPU的依赖。

在技术原理上，Jalapeño作为ASIC，其架构针对AI推理工作负载进行了深度定制，通过硬件级的优化来加速大规模语言模型的实时响应。与通用GPU相比，ASIC在特定任务上能实现更高的效率和更低的功耗。博通CEO霍克·谭（Hock Tan）在接受路透社采访时表示，Jalapeño的性能已与英伟达的Blackwell芯片和谷歌的张量处理单元（TPU）相匹敌。OpenAI强调，虽然最终性能仍在评估中，但早期测试显示，Jalapeño在每瓦性能（performance per watt）上“显著优于当前最先进水平”。这体现了其核心创新在于能效比的突破——在同等功耗下提供更高的推理吞吐量。

目前，微软、Meta、亚马逊等AI巨头也已陆续推出自研AI服务器芯片，用于训练或推理，但在整体性能上仍落后于英伟达的GPU。OpenAI将Jalapeño称为“多代计算平台的第一步”，计划于2026年底前部署。这款芯片的推出不仅有助于OpenAI降低对外部供应商的依赖，还可能重新定义数据中心推理负载的能效标准。应用场景上，它将直接服务于OpenAI旗下的大语言模型（如GPT系列）及基于Agent的实时交互服务，同时为未来更高带宽、更低延迟的AI基础设施奠定基础。随着更多企业加入自研芯片行列，AI算力市场的竞争格局正从单一GPU主导走向多元定制化，而Jalapeño的能效优势可能成为推动这一转变的关键变量。

items.title: SK hynix files to raise up to $29 billion in historic Nasdaq listing — all proceeds going to advanced AI memory fabs and EUV tool orders
items.description: SK hynix filed a securities registration statement on Wednesday to raise up to 45.45 trillion won through an American depositary receipt listing on Nasdaq.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SK hynix filed a securities registration statement with South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service and the U.S. SEC on Wednesday to raise up to 45.45 trillion won ($29.43 billion) through an American depositary receipt listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market scheduled for July 10th — and the company said the whole lot would go toward investments in factories and equipment.The offering covers 17.79 million newly issued common shares and would rank among the largest ADR sales ever completed. SK hynix earmarked the proceeds for its first fab in the Yongin cluster, an advanced packaging plant in Cheongju, and chipmaking equipment that includes EUV scanners.None of the funded projects will produce memory in time to ease the shortage that’s still driving up prices. SK hynix holds about 57% of the HBM market and 32% of global DRAM, and chairman Chey Tae-won has said repeatedly that AI demand will keep supply tight until 2030.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The filing names three target projects that SK has already committed to and is now financing via public markets. The first fab in the Yongin Semiconductor Cluster, designated Y1, carries a 31 trillion won ($21.5 billion) commitment for its initial phase and is due for completion around February 2027, with equipment installation to follow in the second quarter. The Cheongju P&T7 advanced packaging plant, a 19 trillion won ($12.9 billion) site dedicated to HBM assembly and testing, broke ground in April and should be completed at the end of 2027. EUV lithography is needed in both, and SK hynix placed a record $7.9 billion order with ASML in March covering roughly 30 scanners through 2027.10 ADRs will represent one common share, with the final per-ADR price to be set through bookbuilding shortly before the July 10th debut, and SK hynix said the total raise could change from the 45.45 trillion won ceiling. BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, and JP Morgan are managing the sale, and the regulatory review is expected to conclude by July 3rd, with the new shares registering on the Korea Exchange on July 29th. You may like SK hynix to double memory wafer capacity within five years, chairman says SK hynix customers offer to buy its EUV machines and fund new fab lines as memory capacity hits zero amid crushing AI-driven shortages SK hynix passes Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company Both the Yongin fab and the Cheongju packaging plant reach volume output in 2027, near the back end of that window. The company confirmed the ADR plan in early June alongside a pledge to double wafer capacity within five years, a timeline Chey acknowledged would do little to shorten the squeeze. DRAM contract prices have run higher through 2026 as the three memory makers tilt wafer capacity toward HBM, which consumes around three times the silicon per gigabyte of standard DDR5.Just two days ago, SK hynix passed Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company, ending a 26-year run at the top. Its Korea-listed shares rose 5.5% in after-hours trading following the disclosure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: SK海力士申请历史性纳斯达克上市，募资高达290亿美元——全部收益将投入先进AI内存晶圆厂和EUV光刻工具订单
items.descriptionZh:
SK海力士于本周三向韩国金融监督院和美国证券交易委员会（SEC）提交了证券注册声明，计划通过在美国纳斯达克全球精选市场发行美国存托凭证（ADR）募集最高达45.45万亿韩元（约合294.3亿美元）的资金。此次上市定于7月10日进行，涉及新增发行1779万股普通股，将成为有史以来规模最大的ADR发行之一。公司明确表示，全部募集资金将用于工厂和设备投资。

**募资用途与核心技术布局：** 资金将专项用于三个已启动的重大项目：1）龙仁半导体集群的首座晶圆厂（代号Y1），一期投资31万亿韩元（约215亿美元），预计2027年2月完工，随后在第二季度进行设备安装；2）清州的P&T7先进封装工厂，投资19万亿韩元（约129亿美元），专用于HBM（高带宽存储器）的组装与测试，已于4月破土动工，预计2027年底建成；3）采购多台EUV（极紫外）光刻机——SK海力士已于3月向ASML下达了创纪录的79亿美元订单，涵盖2027年前交付的约30台扫描仪。EUV光刻技术是制造先进DRAM和HBM的核心环节，能够实现更精细的电路图案，提升芯片性能和能效。

**市场地位与性能参数：** SK海力士目前占据全球HBM市场约57%的份额，以及全球DRAM市场32%的份额。董事长崔泰源多次表示，AI（人工智能）驱动的需求将使存储器供应紧张持续到2030年。不过，上述投资项目投产时间均在2027年左右，无法缓解当前因HBM产能挤占而持续推高的DRAM价格。事实上，由于三大存储器制造商将晶圆产能转向HBM（每GB HBM消耗的硅片面积约为标准DDR5的三倍），DRAM合约价格预计将上涨至2026年。此外，SK海力士两天前刚刚超越三星电子，成为韩国市值最高的上市公司，结束了三星长达26年的榜首地位，其韩国上市股票在公告后盘后交易中上涨5.5%。

**技术影响与应用场景：** 此次大规模融资与产能扩张直接回应了AI大模型训练和推理对高带宽、低功耗存储器的爆发式需求。HBM作为GPU（图形处理器）的关键配套芯片，是AI服务器中的核心部件。SK海力士承诺在五年内将存储器晶圆产能翻倍，但承认这一时间表对缓解短期供需缺口作用有限。ADR发行的具体定价将通过7月10日前的簿记建档确定，每10份ADR代表1股普通股，总募资金额可能根据最终结果调整。美银证券、花旗、高盛和摩根大通担任承销商，监管审查预计于7月3日结束，新股将于7月29日在韩国交易所登记上市。

items.title: AI data center boom hits a human bottleneck — critical skilled labor shortages could slow deployment despite billions in funding
items.description: Data center construction is facing many challenges, and among them is a shortage of skilled hands to assemble the things.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The AI boom has already been responsible for soaring demand and subsequent shortages in the areas of GPUs, computer memory, storage (both spinning and solid-state), electrical power, water, and networking equipment. The latest bottleneck may be the people needed to build the data centers themselves.Asked by Bloomberg TV whether demand for data center construction was slowing, construction industry CEO Benoit Bazin said activity remains strong, but then identified labor as one of the industry's key bottlenecks. The executive, whose company Saint-Gobain supplies construction materials and building products used in hundreds of such projects, argued that labor shortages are already affecting projects in North America and are beginning to emerge in Europe as well.Bazin mentioned the issue only in passing during his Bloomberg appearance, but his comments point toward what is becoming an increasingly important challenge for the AI infrastructure boom. The global race to build new computing infrastructure has hyperscalers like Amazon, Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Oracle collectively committing hundreds of billions of dollars toward new facilities, but constructing a modern AI data center requires far more than just money.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: AI's demand for power is so great that the Three Mile Island nuclear plant, decomissioned in 2019, is set to reopen to serve Microsoft exclusively. (Image credit: Getty / Bloomberg)As we've reported many times before, power availability is the primary constraint facing new projects. Electrical substations, transformers, transmission infrastructure, utility connections, and even generation capacity itself are already struggling to keep pace with demand. However, a growing number of executives and analysts now argue, like Bazin, that skilled labor may be emerging as a significant secondary bottleneck.You see, unlike conventional commercial construction projects, data centers require large numbers of specialized workers. You can't simply rely on standard commercial construction crews for this stuff; you need highly specialized tradesmen, like electricians, high-voltage technicians, fiber-optic installers, HVAC specialists, controls engineers, and commissioning teams, among many others. Huge swaths of these jobs require years of training and experience, making it difficult for the labor pool to expand as quickly as AI investment has ballooned. You may like Exploding number of AI data center build-outs delay Texas housing projects Analytics group signals possible delays at 40% of AI data center construction sites Half of planned US data center builds have been delayed or canceled, growth limited by shortages of power infrastructure and parts from China The problem has become serious enough that some technology companies have begun funding workforce development efforts directly. Earlier this year, Meta partnered with CBRE on a training initiative intended to help expand the pipeline of workers qualified for data center construction and operations, reflecting concerns that labor shortages could eventually slow deployment schedules.The effects may already be spilling into other sectors. We recently highlighted how demand from large data center projects has increased competition for electricians in Texas, contributing to delays in some residential housing developments as contractors struggle to compete with the wages and budgets offered by hyperscaler-backed projects. While housing obviously won't be displaced entirely, that story is an example of how AI infrastructure spending is increasingly competing for the same pool of skilled tradespeople needed elsewhere in the economy.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Citizens gather at a rally to oppose the construction of new data centers in St. Paul, Minnesota. (Image credit: Getty Images / Universal Images Group)Also, labor is only one of several non-technical challenges facing new projects. Public opposition has become increasingly visible in some communities, particularly as residents raise concerns about electricity consumption, water usage, noise, and the broader impact of large-scale data center developments. Looking again to Texas, where numerous projects have been proposed or announced, opposition to new facilities has become a recurring topic of debate. Concerns that once focused primarily on industrial facilities and energy projects are increasingly being directed toward data centers as well.Demand for new facilities remains strong, and few observers actually expect overall construction activity to slow significantly in the near term, but building the infrastructure required to support the next generation of AI systems means solving a growing list of problems, from power generation and grid capacity to permitting, community opposition, and now, increasingly, workforce shortages. The industry has largely solved the problem of attracting capital. It can order more GPUs, buy more land, and sign larger power contracts. Producing thousands of experienced electricians and technicians, however, takes years. As the global data center boom continues, that shortage may prove to be one of the industry's most stubborn constraints. items.titleZh: AI数据中心热潮遭遇人力瓶颈——关键技能劳动力短缺可能减缓部署，尽管有数十亿美元资金投入
items.descriptionZh:
人工智能（AI）产业的爆发式增长已导致GPU、计算机内存、存储设备（包括机械硬盘和固态硬盘）、电力、水资源以及网络设备等领域的需求飙升和相继短缺。如今，建设数据中心所需的人力资源可能成为最新的瓶颈。在接受彭博电视采访时，建筑材料巨头圣戈班（Saint-Gobain）的首席执行官伯努瓦·巴赞（Benoit Bazin）表示，尽管数据中心建设活动依然强劲，但劳动力已成为该行业的关键瓶颈之一。圣戈班为全球数百个数据中心项目提供建筑材料和产品，巴赞指出，劳动力短缺已影响北美项目，并开始在欧洲显现。巴赞的评论暗示了AI基础设施繁荣面临的一个日益严峻的挑战：全球竞相建设新的计算基础设施，亚马逊、微软、谷歌、Meta和甲骨文等超大规模云服务商已承诺投入数千亿美元，但现代化AI数据中心的建设远不止需要资金。

此前报道已多次强调，电力供应是新建项目面临的首要制约因素。变电站、变压器、输电基础设施、电网接入甚至发电能力本身已难以跟上需求。然而，越来越多的企业高管和分析师认为，技术工人正成为重要的二级瓶颈。与常规商业建筑项目不同，数据中心需要大量专业人才：不能仅靠普通建筑工人，而必须依赖高度专业化的技工，如电工、高压技术员、光纤安装工、暖通空调专家、控制工程师和调试团队等。这些岗位中的大部分需要多年培训和经验，使得劳动力储备难以像AI投资那样快速扩张。问题已严重到一些科技公司直接资助劳动力发展项目——今年早些时候，Meta与世邦魏理仕（CBRE）合作推出培训计划，旨在扩大合格的数据中心建设和运营工人队伍，反映出对劳动力短缺可能拖慢部署进度的担忧。

影响已开始外溢到其他经济领域。例如，德州大型数据中心项目对电工的需求加剧了竞争，导致部分住宅开发项目因承包商难以与超大规模云商支持的预算和工资竞争而延期。虽然住宅建设不会被完全挤占，但这表明AI基础设施投入正与经济的其他部分争夺同一批熟练技工。此外，劳动力只是新项目面临的非技术挑战之一。公众反对日益明显，居民对电力消耗、用水、噪音以及大型数据中心开发的影响表示担忧。同样在德州，多个提议或宣布的项目引发了反复的辩论，过去主要针对工业设施和能源项目的反对声，如今也指向数据中心。

对新设施的需求依然强劲，很少有观察者预期短期内的整体建设活动会显著放缓。但支撑下一代AI系统所需的基础设施建设意味着要解决越来越多的问题：从发电和电网容量、许可审批、社区反对，到现在日益凸显的劳动力短缺。行业已基本解决了吸引资本的问题——可以订购更多GPU、购买更多土地、签订更大规模的电力合同——但培养数千名经验丰富的电工和技术人员需要数年时间。随着全球数据中心建设热潮持续，劳动力短缺可能成为该行业最顽固的约束之一。

items.titleZh: 立即以214美元入手32GB海盗船DDR5内存，得益于Newegg捆绑套装——AMD与Intel游戏PC组装套件大幅低于350美元市场价
items.descriptionZh:
Newegg在Prime Day期间推出了两套极具性价比的AMD和Intel平台内存套装优惠，让玩家能以远低于市场价的价格获得32GB DDR5-6000高速内存。这两套组合并非简单的单品打包，而是通过巧妙的产品搭配，将内存的实际成本压缩至约215美元（约合人民币1500元）左右，相比单独购买同规格内存节省了至少135美元。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
DDR5内存相比上一代DDR4，核心改进在于更高的数据传输速率和更低的电压。此次套装中的海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000内存（CL时序未明确但属于主流水平）运行在6000 MT/s（兆传输/秒）频率下，是当前AM5平台游戏玩家的甜点频率。它采用双通道配置（2×16GB），双通道技术允许CPU同时访问两根内存条，使内存带宽翻倍，从而显著提升游戏帧率和游戏加载速度。海盗船复仇者系列还拥有十区域可编程RGB灯效，支持个性化光效设置。

### 性能参数与对比数据
**AMD套装**：技嘉X870 Aorus Elite Wi-Fi 7冰霜白主板（价值约225美元）搭配海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000 32GB（2×16GB）内存，总价479.99美元，折算内存仅215美元。主板基于AM5插槽，支持最新AMD锐龙处理器（如9800X3D），提供12个后置USB接口（含两个40Gbps USB-C）、四个M.2固态硬盘插槽（其中三个支持PCIe 5.0 x4，一个支持PCIe 4.0），配备2.5G有线网卡和Wi-Fi 7无线网卡，最大可安装256GB DDR5内存（频率可达8200 MT/s）。主板通体白色，适合搭建全白主题主机。

**Intel套装**：技嘉Z890 Aorus Master Ultra Core主板（支持LGA 1851插槽，适配最新Arrow Lake架构酷睿Ultra 270K Plus等处理器）搭配同规格黑色版海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000 32GB内存，总价563.98美元，折算内存仅214美元（比AMD版便宜1美元）。主板提供14个后置USB接口、5个M.2插槽（其中两个支持PCIe 5.0），配备10G超高速有线网卡、Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4，支持技嘉EZ DIY易用设计辅助装机。

**价格对比**：AMD套装整体节省28%（节省184.99美元），Intel套装整体节省26%（节省200美元）。若单独购买同规格内存，市面上最低价格约为350美元，而此次套装折合内存价格仅约214-215美元，性价比突出。

### 技术影响与应用场景
这两套组合精准覆盖了当前游戏PC的两大主流平台。AMD AM5平台凭借3D V-Cache技术（如9800X3D）在游戏性能上表现卓越，而Intel LGA 1851平台则凭借高频率和多核心优势在创意工作负载中依然强势。DDR5-6000频率恰好适配AMD Zen 4/5架构的FCLK同步机制，能实现最低延迟和最佳游戏帧率。对于追求极致性价比的游戏玩家、想要升级旧平台（DDR4）的用户，以及搭建全白个性化主机的爱好者来说，这些套装是当前Prime Day期间最值得入手的硬件组合。不过优惠随时可能结束，建议有需求的用户尽快下单。

items.title: Meta pauses mandatory AI training program that tracked employee keystrokes after internal data leak exposed sensitive staff information company-wide — employees express frustration over poor handling of data
items.description: Meta has paused an internal AI training program after employee conversations, keystrokes, transcripts, and performance-related data were reportedly exposed across the company.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Meta has suspended an internal AI training program after an internal data leak exposed sensitive employee information company-wide, according to a Business Insider report on June 22. The program, introduced in April, was designed to help Meta train AI systems on real employee workflows by gathering data, but has now triggered internal backlash over privacy and data security.Screenshots obtained by Business Insider showed that data collected through the program was more broadly accessible within Meta than intended. The exposed information reportedly included private employee conversations, performance-related data, transcriptions, and activity records. Meta classified the incident as a SEV 2, on an internal scale of 0 to 5, where SEV 0 is the most severe.A Meta spokesperson confirmed that the company has paused the program while it investigates the incident. "We have carefully designed this program with privacy safeguards, and while we have no indication at this time that any data was improperly accessed by Meta employees, we're pausing it while we investigate," the spokesperson told Business Insider. The incident does not appear to be an external hack but rather an internal data mismanagement.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Meta introduced the program, called the Model Capability Initiative, to monitor employee behavior for use in improving its AI models. The program, which the company reportedly made mandatory for most staff, collected data on employees’ work activities, including keystrokes, mouse movements, conversations, transcripts, and performance-related information.Employees were reportedly uncomfortable with the idea of their keystrokes and mouse movements being recorded for AI training. Now they're finding out the data may not have been properly protected, and was widely accessible across the company rather than restricted to intended viewers. You may like After spat with Chinese gov't, Meta cuts AI Manus off from its internal systems and is 'sunsetting' platform, report claims Amazon employees admit to using AI unnecessarily to pump up internal usage scores Mark Zuckerberg says Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs to pay for AI infrastructure Screenshots reviewed by Business Insider reportedly showed employees criticizing the failure to lock down the data from the start. “I am incensed,” one employee wrote in an internal group, according to the report. Another said there was no evidence of malicious access, but called the lack of promised restrictions “super frustrating.”The episode is the latest in a frustrating stretch for Meta's workforce. The company has cut thousands of jobs in part to fund AI infrastructure behind more powerful AI systems; the same class of systems that Meta and other companies are deploying to replace workers. Building these models also requires vast amounts of training data, and Meta turned to its own employees to supply it, a move most employees were reportedly against. Now these employees have learned that the data they were compelled to hand over was not adequately secured, leaving it exposed to much of the company.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: Meta暂停强制AI培训项目：该项目曾追踪员工键盘输入，因内部数据泄露导致全公司敏感员工信息曝光——员工对糟糕的数据处理方式表示不满
items.descriptionZh:
Meta公司近日因内部数据泄露事件，被迫暂停了一项名为“模型能力倡议”（Model Capability Initiative）的内部AI训练项目。据Business Insider 6月22日报道，该项目自今年4月启动，旨在通过收集员工真实工作流程数据来训练AI系统，但如今却因隐私和数据安全问题引发内部强烈反弹。

该项目设计的初衷是让Meta的AI模型能够从员工日常工作中学习，以提升其智能化水平。具体而言，系统会记录员工的键盘敲击、鼠标移动、对话内容、会议转录以及绩效相关活动数据。Meta声称项目配备了隐私保护措施，但内部泄露的截图显示，这些本应限制在特定观察者范围内的数据，实际上在Meta公司内部被广泛访问，暴露了包括私人对话、绩效数据、转录记录等敏感信息。Meta内部将此次事件定性为SEV 2级（严重程度从0到5，0为最严重），表明问题已相当严重。

Meta发言人向Business Insider证实已暂停该项目，并正在调查。发言人表示：“我们精心设计了隐私保护措施，目前没有证据表明Meta员工不当访问了数据，但为了安全起见，我们暂停项目。”值得注意的是，这并非外部黑客攻击，而是内部数据管理失误。此前，Meta曾要求大多数员工强制参与该项目，许多员工本已对记录自己的键盘和鼠标活动感到不安，如今得知数据未得到妥善保护，愤怒情绪迅速蔓延。内部群组中，有员工直言“我气疯了”，也有人指出没有发现恶意访问，但缺乏承诺中的访问限制“令人极其沮丧”。

这一事件对Meta员工而言是雪上加霜。近年来Meta已多次大规模裁员，部分原因正是为了筹集资金建设更强大的AI基础设施——而这类AI系统正被公司部署来替代人类工作。构建这些模型需要海量训练数据，Meta转向自己员工“供应”数据，多数员工本就反对，如今又发现被迫上交的数据竟然安全漏洞百出，几乎向全公司开放。

从技术影响看，该事件暴露了AI训练数据采集中的核心伦理困境：当企业将员工行为数据作为训练素材时，如何在模型性能提升与个人隐私保护之间取得平衡？Meta的“模型能力倡议”本质上是一种“监控式学习”，试图从人类工作流程中提炼模式，但若数据访问权限设计不当，极易引发信任危机。应用场景上，这类内部数据训练法原本可能推广至其他企业AI项目，但此次泄露无疑会促使行业重新审视数据治理的边界。

目前Meta尚未公布后续改进措施，但这一事件已为整个科技行业敲响警钟：AI的进化不能以牺牲内部数据安全为代价，否则再强大的模型也将失去员工的信任基石。

items.title: NVIDIA and AWS Collaborate to Bring AI to Production at Scale
items.description: Building AI systems at scale is demanding, requiring low-latency inference, fast vector search, strong GPU price-performance and infrastructure that can grow without multiplying operational complexity.  NVIDIA’s latest work with Amazon Web Services (AWS) addresses each of those constraints. Across Amazon OpenSearch and Amazon EC2, NVIDIA AI infrastructure is giving enterprises more practical paths to deploy […]
items.content: Building AI systems at scale is demanding, requiring low-latency inference, fast vector search, strong GPU price-performance and infrastructure that can grow without multiplying operational complexity. NVIDIA’s latest work with Amazon Web Services (AWS) addresses each of those constraints. Across Amazon OpenSearch and Amazon EC2, NVIDIA AI infrastructure is giving enterprises more practical paths to deploy AI at production scale. EC2 G7 instances powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs expand the compute layer for AI, graphics, video and data analytics workloads, while the NVIDIA cuVS library accelerates the retrieval layer by making GPU-powered vector indexing the default in OpenSearch Serverless. And with AWS achieving NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud status for NVIDIA GB300, customers can trust they’re receiving peak optimized performance for their training workloads. NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition Multi-Workload GPUs Power New Amazon EC2 G7 Instances Amazon EC2 G7 instances bring NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs to AWS for AI inference, graphics, spatial computing and GPU-accelerated data analytics — delivering a new instance type engineered for production workloads that need performance without the operational overhead of a customer-managed GPU platform. Compared with G6 instances, G7 delivers up to 4.6x AI inference performance, up to 2.1x graphics performance and significantly faster GPU-accelerated data analytics on Amazon EMR using the NVIDIA cuDF library for Apache Spark workloads. With support for up to eight GPUs, 256GB of total GPU memory, 700 Gbps of EFA-enabled networking and up to 7.6TB of local NVMe SSD storage — across one-, two-, four- and eight- GPU configurations plus bare metal, coming soon — G7 instances let customers right-size infrastructure for their workloads instead of over-provisioning for them. The platform’s versatility means AI teams get lower-latency inference. Media and entertainment teams get high-resolution video workflows and rendering. Simulation, computer-aided design, virtual desktop infrastructure, gaming and spatial computing teams get the same instance type for graphics-intensive applications. And data teams can apply the GPU memory, local storage and networking improvements to analytics pipelines and vector database workloads. G7 instances are accessible through AWS Deep Learning Amazon Machine Images (AMIs), Amazon Deep Learning Containers, Amazon EMR, Amazon EKS, Amazon ECS and graphics AMIs — and coming soon to Amazon SageMaker AI. NVIDIA cuVS Makes GPU-Accelerated Vector Search the Default in Amazon OpenSearch The next generation of Amazon OpenSearch Serverless powers agentic AI and dynamic workloads with no infrastructure management required. It uses GPU-accelerated vector indexing, powered by NVIDIA cuVS, as the default compute choice for all vector collections. For teams building retrieval-augmented generation, semantic search, recommendation systems and agentic AI applications, that shift matters. It turns GPU-powered vector search from a specialized optimization project into a standard AWS capability. The customer impact is direct: vector indexing up to 10x faster at a quarter of the cost, compared with CPU-only builds — making billion-scale vector databases practical to build in under an hour. By making NVIDIA cuVS the default in OpenSearch Serverless, AWS customers get a much faster path from raw data to production-ready AI retrieval infrastructure — with serverless scaling that reduces operational overhead when workloads are idle. AWS Achieves NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud Status for GB300 Training Performance AWS has achieved NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud status on NVIDIA GB300 for training workloads. This means AWS meets the rigorous performance thresholds that NVIDIA uses to benchmark AI workloads against its reference architecture. This achievement is the result of deep co-engineering efforts between AWS and NVIDIA teams. Through the NVIDIA Exemplar Clouds initiative, developers and AI leaders can be confident they’re using consistent, high-performance cloud infrastructure for large-scale training, helping teams evaluate cloud providers with greater confidence, improve total cost of ownership and move AI projects from planning to production more efficiently. Together, these advancements reinforce every layer of the AI infrastructure stack on AWS. The throughline is the same: production-grade AI infrastructure that performs at scale, without adding operational burden to the teams running it. Learn more in this AWS blog.
items.author: Josiah Byers
items.titleZh: NVIDIA与AWS合作将AI大规模投入生产
items.descriptionZh:
随着企业级AI部署从实验阶段走向规模化生产，基础设施的延迟、成本、可扩展性和运维复杂度成为关键瓶颈。NVIDIA与Amazon Web Services（AWS）的最新合作，正是针对这些痛点，从计算层、检索层到训练层，系统性优化AI基础设施。此次发布涵盖三大核心更新：基于NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU的新Amazon EC2 G7实例、将GPU加速向量搜索默认集成到Amazon OpenSearch Serverless的NVIDIA cuVS库，以及AWS凭借NVIDIA GB300获得NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证。这三项进展共同为企业在AWS上构建生产级AI提供了更高效、更低成本、更少运维负担的路径。

**NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU驱动新Amazon EC2 G7实例**

Amazon EC2 G7实例是专门为需要高性能但不愿承担自管理GPU平台运维负担的工作负载而设计的新实例类型。它搭载NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU，支持AI推理、图形处理、空间计算和GPU加速数据分析。与上一代G6实例相比，G7在AI推理性能上提升高达4.6倍，图形性能提升高达2.1倍。在Apache Spark数据分析场景下，借助NVIDIA cuDF库，在Amazon EMR上实现显著更快的GPU加速数据处理。

G7实例支持1、2、4、8GPU配置及即将推出的裸金属选项，单实例最多可配备8个GPU，总计256GB GPU显存，700 Gbps的EFA（弹性光纤适配器）网络带宽，以及高达7.6TB的本地NVMe SSD存储。这种灵活的配置使得企业可以根据工作负载精确选择计算资源，避免过度预配带来的成本浪费。

该平台的多工作负载通用性意味着不同团队可以在同一实例类型上获得针对性优化：AI团队获得更低延迟的推理；媒体和娱乐团队能处理高分辨率视频工作流及渲染；仿真、CAD、虚拟桌面基础设施、游戏和空间计算团队可运行图形密集型应用；数据团队则能利用GPU显存、本地存储和网络改进，加速分析管线和向量数据库工作负载。

在生态集成方面，G7实例可通过AWS Deep Learning AMIs、Amazon Deep Learning Containers、Amazon EMR、Amazon EKS、Amazon ECS及图形AMIs访问，并即将在Amazon SageMaker AI中提供。这种广泛的平台支持大大降低了企业的迁移和部署门槛。

**NVIDIA cuVS使GPU加速向量搜索成为Amazon OpenSearch Serverless的默认选项**

新一代Amazon OpenSearch Serverless旨在支持代理式AI和动态工作负载，而无需用户管理任何基础设施。其关键变化是：在所有向量集合中，将基于NVIDIA cuVS库的GPU加速向量索引设为默认计算选项。这意味着对于构建检索增强生成（RAG）、语义搜索、推荐系统和代理式AI应用的团队来说，GPU加速向量搜索不再是需要专门优化的项目，而是AWS的标准功能。

用户直接受益的数据是：与纯CPU构建相比，向量索引速度提升高达10倍，而成本仅为四分之一。这使得在不到一小时内构建十亿级规模的向量数据库成为现实。通过将NVIDIA cuVS设为OpenSearch Serverless的默认引擎，AWS客户可以从原始数据更快地走向生产就绪的AI检索基础设施，同时服务器无服务器（Serverless）的自动伸缩特性还能在工作负载空闲时显著降低运维成本。

**AWS凭借NVIDIA GB300训练性能获得NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证**

AWS在NVIDIA GB300训练工作负载上达到了NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证标准。这意味着AWS的云基础设施通过了NVIDIA设定的严格性能门槛，该门槛用于衡量AI训练工作负载是否达到NVIDIA参考架构的水平。这一成果源自AWS与NVIDIA团队的深度协同工程（co-engineering）努力。

通过NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud计划，开发者和AI领导者可以确信他们使用的是与NVIDIA参考架构一致的高性能云基础设施来进行大规模训练。这有助于团队在评估不同云提供商时更有信心，优化总体拥有成本（TCO），并更高效地将AI项目从规划阶段推进到生产阶段。

综合来看，这三项进展强化了AWS上AI基础设施的每一层。核心思路一以贯之：提供能够大规模稳定运行的生产级AI基础设施，同时不给运维团队增加额外负担。企业可以在AWS上获得更低延迟的推理、更快更便宜的向量检索、以及经过认证的高效训练环境，从而加速AI应用的部署与迭代。

items.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
items.description: Oracle is spending billions on data center infrastructure to support AI.
items.content: The growing use of AI contributed to Oracle laying off 21,000 workers in a year, according to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Monday. In its annual regulatory filing for the fiscal year ending May 31, Oracle said it has 141,000 full-time employees. In its 2025 filing, Oracle said it had 162,000 employees. The reported 12.9 percent reduction followed March reports of mass layoffs at the database management software company. “[T]he adoption and deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce,” the filing reads. However, the job cuts are also tied to large capital expenditure to build Oracle’s data center infrastructure to support AI workloads. “The majority of the initiatives undertaken by the 2026 Restructuring Plan were effected to implement our continued emphasis in developing, marketing, selling, and delivering our cloud-based offerings,” this week’s filing reads. Oracle plans to raise $45 billion to $50 billion in 2026 to expand its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure for customers like OpenAI, xAI, AMD, Nvidia, and Meta, it said in February. About half of that funding will come through debt, with the remainder coming from equity. When Oracle announced this, investors had already been concerned about Oracle’s growing debt to fuel its AI efforts. Overall, Oracle has over $120 billion in debt, per its fiscal year 2026 earnings report. In February, bondholders sued Oracle, claiming that they lost money because Oracle hid the need to raise its debt to build its AI infrastructure, Reuters reported. Investors have also been concerned about Oracle’s reliance on OpenAI, a customer that is not yet profitable and is reportedly losing billions of dollars a year. Analysts noted that Oracle’s workforce reduction will help the company’s cash flow. In March, Barclays said that Oracle makes less profit per employee than its rivals, CNBC reported at the time. In its SEC filing, Oracle said it spent $1.8 billion on restructuring costs in its fiscal year, which is a 481 percent increase from the prior fiscal year’s $374 million. Oracle also noted the drawbacks frequently associated with mass layoffs, including the potential for “reduced productivity” and “shortages of sufficiently skilled employees in certain roles, loss of valuable institutional knowledge, and damage to employee morale and retention.” “As our cloud and AI businesses grow, we will continually balance our resources and restructure our development group to help ensure we have the right people delivering the best cloud and AI products to our customers around the world,” Oracle said in a statement to CNBC. While generative AI has reignited concerns about AI taking over jobs, Oracle demonstrates one way AI can contribute to job losses beyond direct human replacements. That said, AI is increasingly common for companies to cite when letting workers go. “AI is now the leading reason companies give for cutting jobs, and the primary industry citing it is technology,” Andy Challenger, CRO at outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas, said in a May 2026 report released in June. “Technology, already the year’s biggest job cutter, saw its steepest cuts since early 2023, even as it remains the sector with the most hiring plans this year.” The outplacement firm reported in January that AI had been cited for 71,825 “job cut announcements” from 2023 to 2025.
items.author: Scharon Harding
items.titleZh: 甲骨文裁员21000人助力其债务驱动的人工智能投资
items.descriptionZh:
根据美国证券交易委员会（SEC）周一提交的文件，甲骨文（Oracle）在过去一年中因AI的广泛应用裁员2.1万人。在截至5月31日的2026财年年度监管文件中，甲骨文称其全职员工总数为14.1万人，而2025财年该数字为16.2万人，同比下降12.9%。这一裁员比例紧随3月份关于该数据库管理软件公司大规模裁员的报道。文件明确指出：“AI技术在我们运营中的采用和部署已经导致，并可能继续导致员工数量的减少。”然而，裁员也与甲骨文为支持AI工作负载而建设数据中心基础设施所需的大量资本支出密切相关。本周提交的文件称：“2026年重组计划所采取的大部分举措，都是为了落实我们对开发、营销、销售和交付云服务产品的持续重点。”

甲骨文在2月表示，计划在2026年筹集450亿至500亿美元，以扩展其Oracle Cloud Infrastructure，服务于OpenAI、xAI、AMD、Nvidia和Meta等客户。其中约一半资金将通过债务筹集，其余来自股权。当甲骨文宣布这一计划时，投资者早已对其为AI努力而不断增加债务感到担忧。根据2026财年财报，甲骨文总债务超过1200亿美元。据路透社报道，今年2月，债券持有人起诉甲骨文，声称因甲骨文隐瞒了为AI基础设施提高债务的必要性而遭受损失。投资者还担心甲骨文对尚未盈利且据报道每年亏损数十亿美元的客户OpenAI的依赖。

分析师指出，裁员将有助于改善甲骨文的现金流。今年3月，CNBC报道称巴克莱银行表示甲骨文每位员工的利润低于竞争对手。在SEC文件中，甲骨文披露本财年重组支出达18亿美元，较上一财年的3.74亿美元增长481%。甲骨文还指出了大规模裁员常伴随的弊端，包括“生产率下降”、“某些岗位严重缺乏足够技能的员工、宝贵机构知识的流失，以及员工士气和留存率受损”。甲骨文在给CNBC的声明中表示：“随着我们的云和AI业务增长，我们将持续平衡资源并重组开发团队，以确保拥有合适的人才，为全球客户提供最佳的云和AI产品。”

虽然生成式AI重新引发了AI取代工作的担忧，但甲骨文展示了一种AI导致失业的途径——并非直接替代人类，而是通过推动公司重组和资源再分配。与此同时，越来越多的公司在解雇员工时引用AI作为理由。职业转型公司Challenger, Gray & Christmas在2026年5月的报告（6月发布）中指出：“AI现在已成为公司裁员的首要原因，而引用最多的行业是科技行业。”首席执行官Andy Challenger表示：“科技行业已经是今年裁员最多的行业，其裁员幅度自2023年初以来最为剧烈，尽管该行业仍是今年招聘计划最多的领域。”该职业转型公司在1月报告称，2023年至2025年期间，AI已被引用为71,825次“裁员公告”的原因。

history.title: Broadcom and OpenAI unveil custom-built Jalapeño inference processor — OpenAI's first chip is a massive reticle-sized ASIC built in an ultra-fast nine-month development cycle
history.description: Broadcom and OpenAI reveal their Jalapeño custom-built inference ASIC that allegedly beats existing leading-edge in terms of performance-per-watt.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter OpenAI and Broadcom have introduced Jalapeño, a custom-built inference processor designed specifically for modern large language models and future agentic AI workloads, which is designed to deliver performance per watt they claim is higher than today's leading-edge hardware. OpenAI considers its hardware project a strategic one and envisions Jalapeño to be the first generation of its inference hardware.Not another AI acceleratorOpenAI stresses that Jalapeño is a purpose-built inference ASIC and not a repurposed training accelerator or a general-purpose AI processor. OpenAI says the architecture of Jalapeño was designed based on its understanding of LLM behavior and is meant to address practical bottlenecks that matter for inference at scale, including costly data movement, balance between compute and memory resources, networking efficiency, and overall behavior. OpenAI also states that the design of the processor is meant to wed high throughput with low latency (which is why it uses a huge compute chiplet and HBM memory and not cheaper types of DRAM like many other inference accelerators), which will be particularly handy for reasoning and agentic workloads.In addition, OpenAI and Broadcom claim the processor is built to deliver higher effective utilization than conventional AI accelerators and deliver performance that is close to the theoretical maximum, which means very high efficiency both in terms of costs and in terms of power. Meanwhile, the companies did not disclose performance targets for their Jalapeño ASIC, so these claims should be taken with a grain of salt.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Engineering samples are already operating in the lab at target clock speed and power (though Broadcom and OpenAI do not disclose details about this, either), and OpenAI says it is running machine learning workloads, such as GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark.The two companies also claim that early internal testing indicates that Jalapeño's performance-per-watt is substantially better than 'current state-of-the-art hardware,' although no hard numbers, benchmarks, memory configuration, or other details are disclosed, so again, we will have to take the claims with a grain of salt. In addition, one must bear in mind that while Jalapeño can purportedly beat existing AMD's Instinct MI350-series and Nvidia's Blackwell-based accelerators, it remains to be seen how competitive it will be against AMD's Instinct MI400-series and Nvidia's Rubin-based offerings. You may like The custom AI ASIC state of play (May 2026) — Broadcom deals, Google TPUs, Meta MTIA & beyond Inside Google's TPU V8 strategy, delivering two chips for two crucial tasks at incredible scale Broadcom to supply Meta with custom silicon through 2029 "Jalapeño was designed from the ground up for LLM inference using detailed insights from our close collaboration with OpenAI researchers," said Richard Ho, who leads OpenAI's hardware program. "We optimized the architecture around the kernels, memory movement, networking, and serving patterns that matter most for frontier AI models. Based on early testing, Jalapeño will efficiently execute our most important workloads close to the hardware’s theoretical limits."A massive chip with six HBM modulesWhile Broadcom and OpenAI did not disclose specifications of Jalapeño, they did show its wafer and packaging, so we can do a brief analysis. The package appears to contain one large compute chiplet surrounded by six HBM modules and another chiplet that likely packs input/output interfaces and is surrounded by two structural dummy dies.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: OpenAI)The wafer image does look like a Broadcom-style systolic-array-heavy accelerator, in the sense that it shows a very regular, repeated, columnar floorplan with what looks like replicated compute regions and fixed infrastructure macros. Yet, keep in mind that we are speculating, and the image is not clean enough to say that this is definitely Broadcom's standard TPU-like systolic array template with some perks from OpenAI,From the image alone, it is impossible to tell whether Jalapeño uses a true 2D systolic array, a set of 1D/2D matrix engines, a collection of vector or tensor tiles, or some other inference datapath. All we can say is that the die has a highly repetitive floorplan consistent with several kinds of tiled AI accelerator architectures. (Image credit: OpenAI)What we can tell from the image is the approximate die size of Jalapeño's compute chiplet based on the size of HBM3/4 packages (10.975 mm × 10.975 mm) that surround it. From what we can tell, the chiplet measures 25.46 mm (width) × 33 mm (height), which means that its die size is around 840 mm2, which is very close to the reticle size of EUV lithography systems (858 mm2). Given that the quality of the shot is poor, the die size we estimate cannot be 100% accurate, but we suspect it is close enough. What to read next Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 Meta's multi-billion-dollar Graviton deal highlights intensifying CPU shortages in AI infrastructure Intel and SambaNova team up on heterogenous AI inference platform — different hardware performs different workloads The die size of Jalapeño's compute chiplet implies that it packs quite a lot of compute oomph, though, of course, we cannot make performance estimates based on this metric. Yet, it is safe to say that Jalapeño's compute die is considerably bigger than compute dies of other inference accelerators on the market and more resembles processors for AI training. Speaking of processors for AI training, we increasingly see multi-chiplet designs for these workloads as companies like AMD and Nvidia want to pack as much performance as possible. Meanwhile, the fact that OpenAI and Broadcom chose to go with a large compute chiplet possibly indicates that they wanted to reduce latencies by as much as possible.Designed in nine monthsThe companies say the chip reached tape-out in just nine months and is slated for deployment beginning in late 2026, which represents an extremely fast turnaround time in ASIC design. It is unclear whether Broadcom and OpenAI extensively used artificial intelligence to define and then develop Jalapeño, though the companies admitted that they used OpenAI's models to speed up parts of the chip's design and optimization work. Typically, it takes 1.5 – 2 years to design an ASIC from scratch, so AI can shrink the development cycle. Another means to accelerate the design cycle is Broadcom's extensive reuse of its logic across different custom designs to deliver new chips faster than other companies.It is noteworthy that, according to the announcement, Jalapeño is designed to support not only OpenAI's own workloads but also present and future LLMs across the industry, which potentially lets OpenAI sell its hardware to third parties, assuming that it can get enough supply from Broadcom and TSMC. Meanwhile, the chief executive of Broadcom indicates that Jalapeño will be deployed at gigawatt-scale data centers with Microsoft and other partners starting this year, though it is unclear whether the processor will be used exclusively for OpenAI workloads or will be available for other tenants as well."Our collaboration with OpenAI represents a fundamental commitment to scaling the physical infrastructure required for the next decade of AI," said Hock Tan, President and CEO, Broadcom. "This is just the beginning of a multi-generation roadmap. By co-developing our industry-leading silicon directly with OpenAI, we are enabling the deployment of gigawatt-scale data centers with Microsoft and other partners beginning in 2026." history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 博通与OpenAI联合发布定制Jalapeño推理处理器——OpenAI首款芯片是巨大的掩模版级ASIC，开发周期仅九个月，速度极快
history.descriptionZh:
OpenAI与Broadcom联合推出了名为Jalapeño的定制化推理处理器，这款芯片专为现代大语言模型（LLM）及未来的智能体AI（agentic AI）工作负载而设计。OpenAI强调，Jalapeño并非重新利用的训练加速器或通用AI处理器，而是一款纯粹为推理而生的专用ASIC（专用集成电路）。其架构基于OpenAI对LLM行为的深刻理解，旨在解决大规模推理中的实际瓶颈，包括昂贵的数据移动、计算与内存资源的平衡、网络效率以及整体运行行为。为了实现高吞吐量与低延迟的结合（这对推理和智能体任务尤为关键），Jalapeño采用了一个巨大的计算芯粒并搭配HBM高带宽内存，而非许多其他推理加速器所使用的廉价DRAM。

OpenAI和Broadcom声称，Jalapeño能够实现比传统AI加速器更高的有效利用率，其性能接近理论极限，这意味着在成本和功耗方面都具备极高效率。不过，双方并未披露具体的性能目标，因此这些声明需要谨慎看待。据透露，工程样品已在实验室中以目标时钟频率和功耗运行（具体细节同样未公开），OpenAI表示它正在运行GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark等机器学习负载。早期内部测试显示，Jalapeño的每瓦性能“显著优于当前最先进的硬件”，但未提供具体数字、基准测试或内存配置等细节。此外，虽然Jalapeño据称能击败现有的AMD Instinct MI350系列和Nvidia Blackwell加速器，但它能否与未来的AMD Instinct MI400系列和Nvidia Rubin产品竞争，仍有待观察。

从公开的晶圆和封装图像可以分析，Jalapeño的封装包含一个大型计算芯粒，周围环绕着6个HBM模块（很可能是HBM3或HBM4），以及另一个可能集成输入/输出接口的芯粒，旁边有两个结构性的假晶粒。根据HBM封装尺寸（10.975mm × 10.975mm）推算，该计算芯粒的尺寸约为25.46 mm（宽）× 33 mm（高），面积约840 mm²，非常接近EUV光刻系统的掩模板极限尺寸（858 mm²）。这一尺寸意味着Jalapeño的计算芯粒明显大于市场上其他推理加速器，更接近AI训练处理器的规模。与AMD和Nvidia越来越多采用多芯粒设计以堆叠性能不同，OpenAI和Broadcom选择大型单芯粒，可能主要是为了尽可能降低延迟。

值得注意的是，Jalapeño从设计到流片仅用了9个月，这在ASIC开发中极为迅速。双方承认在芯片设计和优化过程中使用了OpenAI的模型来加速部分工作，这显著缩短了通常需要1.5至2年的开发周期。此外，Broadcom在多个定制设计中对逻辑模块的高度复用也是加快交付的关键。

据公告，Jalapeño不仅支持OpenAI自身的工作负载，还旨在兼容行业当前及未来的LLM，这为OpenAI向第三方销售硬件提供了可能（前提是能从Broadcom和台积电获得足够产能）。Broadcom首席执行官Hock Tan表示，Jalapeño将从今年（2026年）开始部署在微软及其他合作伙伴的千兆瓦级数据中心，具体是仅用于OpenAI工作负载还是也对其他租户开放，尚不明确。Tan强调：“通过与OpenAI共同开发行业领先的硅片，我们正从2026年开始支持千兆瓦级数据中心的部署……这只是一个多代路线图的开始。”

history.title: Figma now has AI motion graphics and shader tools
history.description:
Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives "push their ideas further" and automate tedious tasks with AI. Part of this is a reimagined canvas that's now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials "together in one place."
Notable callouts include coding layers that let you tweak the code of your projects without leaving the Figma Design canvas, and AI-generated motion graphics effects, which allow you to make animations and transition effects by simply describing them to Figma's chatbot interface. You c …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives “push their ideas further” and automate tedious tasks with AI. Part of this is a reimagined canvas that’s now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials “together in one place.”Notable callouts include coding layers that let you tweak the code of your projects without leaving the Figma Design canvas, and AI-generated motion graphics effects, which allow you to make animations and transition effects by simply describing them to Figma’s chatbot interface. You can read more about all the new product updates over on Figma’s website.Otherwise, here’s Figma’s overview of everything that was announced:New creative materials to express ideas● Code layers: Work directly with code on the Figma Design canvas. Clone repositories, generate new directions with Figma’s agent, extract flows into editable design layers, and sync changes back to code.● Motion: Design animations, transitions, and 3D transforms collaboratively, directly from Figma. Prompt to create animations with AI, apply preset styles, or adjust manually on a timeline. Motion is connected to design systems, code-backed, and ready to ship.● Shaders: Prompt to build shader effects and fills, custom visual treatments powered by WebGPU. Effects that weren’t available in Figma before, like dither, pixelate, and various blur types can now be created directly on the canvas.● Figma Weave workflows: Generate consistent and high-quality visuals in Figma with 20 plus integrated Weave tools, turning complex AI workflows into simple tools on the canvas. This is the first step towards a full integration between Figma and Figma Weave, expected later this year.New intelligent tools for teams and agents to collaborate● Agent skills and deeper context: Turn repetitive work into skills that entire teams can use to work more precisely and consistently with Figma’s agent. Bring in more context with third party connectors, web search, and file attachments.● Generative plugins: Extend Figma’s capabilities by building custom, reusable plugins with the agent. Turn prompts into tools that can be tweaked and shared — no developer setup or technical skills required.
history.author: Jess Weatherbed
history.titleZh: Figma 现已推出 AI 动态图形和着色器工具
history.descriptionZh:
在年度Config大会上，Figma发布了多项设计和编码产品更新，旨在帮助创意人员“进一步推动想法”，并借助AI自动化繁琐任务。核心亮点之一是重新设计的画布——据Figma介绍，它已针对全栈开发进行了优化，将团队、AI代理、工具和素材“集中在一个地方”。具体来看，更新涵盖以下两大类别：

### 一、新的创意素材，助力表达想法

1. **编码层（Code layers）**：允许用户在Figma设计画布内直接修改项目代码。用户可以克隆代码仓库、利用Figma的AI代理生成新的方向、将流程提取为可编辑的设计图层，以及将更改同步回代码——无需离开设计环境即可完成开发中的代码调整。
2. **动态效果（Motion）**：直接在Figma中协作设计动画、过渡和3D变换。用户可通过AI提示生成动画、应用预设样式，或在时间轴上手动调整。该功能与设计系统相连、基于代码，并可直接投入生产。
3. **着色器（Shaders）**：通过提示构建着色器效果和填充，利用WebGPU实现自定义视觉处理。此前Figma中无法实现的效果（如抖动、像素化、多种模糊类型）现在可直接在画布上创建。
4. **Figma Weave工作流**：通过20多种集成的Weave工具，在Figma中生成一致且高质量的视觉内容，将复杂的AI工作流转化为画布上的简单工具。这是Figma与Figma Weave全面集成（预计今年晚些时候实现）的第一步。

### 二、面向团队和AI代理的新型智能工具

1. **Agent技能与更深上下文**：将重复性工作转化为技能，整个团队可借助Figma的AI代理更精确、一致地工作。通过第三方连接器、网络搜索和文件附件引入更多上下文。
2. **生成式插件（Generative plugins）**：通过代理构建自定义、可复用的插件来扩展Figma功能。用户可将提示转化为可调整和共享的工具——无需任何开发者环境搭建或技术技能。

此次更新在技术原理上强调“一体化”与“低门槛”：通过将编码、动画、视觉特效及复杂AI工作流深度嵌入设计画布，消除了工具切换的摩擦；AI代理则通过自然语言交互（如聊天界面）将原本需要专业技能的步骤简化为描述性操作。性能方面，虽然没有给出具体数字对比，但“实时同步代码”“提示即生成”等特性显著降低了从设计到开发的时间成本和协作复杂度。应用场景涵盖从UI/UX设计、原型动画、视觉特效制作到团队标准化工作流自动化，尤其适合全栈设计师、产品团队以及希望零代码扩展设计工具能力的非技术成员。更多细节可访问Figma官网查看。

history.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
history.description:
OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday.
Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it's designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI inference, models process a user's request to run an agent like Codex or offer a response from ChatGPT, while AI training involves a model consuming vast amounts of data to inform its responses.
It comes just nine months after OpenAI revealed that it would team up with Br …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: OpenAI has just revealed a new “intelligence processor” chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday.Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it’s designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI inference, models process a user’s request to run an agent like Codex or offer a response from ChatGPT, while AI training involves a model consuming vast amounts of data to inform its responses.It comes just nine months after OpenAI revealed that it would team up with Broadcom to make its own chips, helping reduce the company’s reliance on the GPUs offered by Nvidia that are in limited supply. In an interview with Reuters, Broadcom CEO Hock Tan says it matches the performance of Nvidia’s Blackwell chips and Google’s Tensor processing units. Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon are among the other AI companies that have also launched custom-designed AI chips recently to power their servers for either training or inference, while still trailing Nvidia’s chips on overall performance.OpenAI calls Jalapeño the “first step in a multi-generation compute platform,” which it expects to deploy by the end of 2026. “While OpenAI is still measuring final performance, early testing shows that Jalapeño will deliver performance per watt substantially better than current state-of-the-art,” OpenAI says.
history.author: Emma Roth
history.descriptionZh:
OpenAI近日宣布与博通（Broadcom）合作，共同推出了一款名为“Jalapeño”的新型AI服务器专用芯片，该芯片被定位为“智能处理器”。根据周三的官方公告，Jalapeño是一款专用集成电路（ASIC），专为AI推理任务而设计——即处理用户请求、运行如Codex这样的智能代理或生成ChatGPT响应，而非用于AI训练（训练需要模型消化海量数据以优化回答能力）。这是OpenAI九个月前宣布与博通联手自研芯片后的成果，旨在减少对英伟达（Nvidia）供应紧张的GPU的依赖。

在技术原理上，Jalapeño作为ASIC，其架构针对AI推理工作负载进行了深度定制，通过硬件级的优化来加速大规模语言模型的实时响应。与通用GPU相比，ASIC在特定任务上能实现更高的效率和更低的功耗。博通CEO霍克·谭（Hock Tan）在接受路透社采访时表示，Jalapeño的性能已与英伟达的Blackwell芯片和谷歌的张量处理单元（TPU）相匹敌。OpenAI强调，虽然最终性能仍在评估中，但早期测试显示，Jalapeño在每瓦性能（performance per watt）上“显著优于当前最先进水平”。这体现了其核心创新在于能效比的突破——在同等功耗下提供更高的推理吞吐量。

目前，微软、Meta、亚马逊等AI巨头也已陆续推出自研AI服务器芯片，用于训练或推理，但在整体性能上仍落后于英伟达的GPU。OpenAI将Jalapeño称为“多代计算平台的第一步”，计划于2026年底前部署。这款芯片的推出不仅有助于OpenAI降低对外部供应商的依赖，还可能重新定义数据中心推理负载的能效标准。应用场景上，它将直接服务于OpenAI旗下的大语言模型（如GPT系列）及基于Agent的实时交互服务，同时为未来更高带宽、更低延迟的AI基础设施奠定基础。随着更多企业加入自研芯片行列，AI算力市场的竞争格局正从单一GPU主导走向多元定制化，而Jalapeño的能效优势可能成为推动这一转变的关键变量。

history.title: SK hynix files to raise up to $29 billion in historic Nasdaq listing — all proceeds going to advanced AI memory fabs and EUV tool orders
history.description: SK hynix filed a securities registration statement on Wednesday to raise up to 45.45 trillion won through an American depositary receipt listing on Nasdaq.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SK hynix filed a securities registration statement with South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service and the U.S. SEC on Wednesday to raise up to 45.45 trillion won ($29.43 billion) through an American depositary receipt listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market scheduled for July 10th — and the company said the whole lot would go toward investments in factories and equipment.The offering covers 17.79 million newly issued common shares and would rank among the largest ADR sales ever completed. SK hynix earmarked the proceeds for its first fab in the Yongin cluster, an advanced packaging plant in Cheongju, and chipmaking equipment that includes EUV scanners.None of the funded projects will produce memory in time to ease the shortage that’s still driving up prices. SK hynix holds about 57% of the HBM market and 32% of global DRAM, and chairman Chey Tae-won has said repeatedly that AI demand will keep supply tight until 2030.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The filing names three target projects that SK has already committed to and is now financing via public markets. The first fab in the Yongin Semiconductor Cluster, designated Y1, carries a 31 trillion won ($21.5 billion) commitment for its initial phase and is due for completion around February 2027, with equipment installation to follow in the second quarter. The Cheongju P&T7 advanced packaging plant, a 19 trillion won ($12.9 billion) site dedicated to HBM assembly and testing, broke ground in April and should be completed at the end of 2027. EUV lithography is needed in both, and SK hynix placed a record $7.9 billion order with ASML in March covering roughly 30 scanners through 2027.10 ADRs will represent one common share, with the final per-ADR price to be set through bookbuilding shortly before the July 10th debut, and SK hynix said the total raise could change from the 45.45 trillion won ceiling. BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, and JP Morgan are managing the sale, and the regulatory review is expected to conclude by July 3rd, with the new shares registering on the Korea Exchange on July 29th. You may like SK hynix to double memory wafer capacity within five years, chairman says SK hynix customers offer to buy its EUV machines and fund new fab lines as memory capacity hits zero amid crushing AI-driven shortages SK hynix passes Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company Both the Yongin fab and the Cheongju packaging plant reach volume output in 2027, near the back end of that window. The company confirmed the ADR plan in early June alongside a pledge to double wafer capacity within five years, a timeline Chey acknowledged would do little to shorten the squeeze. DRAM contract prices have run higher through 2026 as the three memory makers tilt wafer capacity toward HBM, which consumes around three times the silicon per gigabyte of standard DDR5.Just two days ago, SK hynix passed Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company, ending a 26-year run at the top. Its Korea-listed shares rose 5.5% in after-hours trading following the disclosure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: SK海力士申请历史性纳斯达克上市，募资高达290亿美元——全部收益将投入先进AI内存晶圆厂和EUV光刻工具订单
history.descriptionZh:
SK海力士于本周三向韩国金融监督院和美国证券交易委员会（SEC）提交了证券注册声明，计划通过在美国纳斯达克全球精选市场发行美国存托凭证（ADR）募集最高达45.45万亿韩元（约合294.3亿美元）的资金。此次上市定于7月10日进行，涉及新增发行1779万股普通股，将成为有史以来规模最大的ADR发行之一。公司明确表示，全部募集资金将用于工厂和设备投资。

**募资用途与核心技术布局：** 资金将专项用于三个已启动的重大项目：1）龙仁半导体集群的首座晶圆厂（代号Y1），一期投资31万亿韩元（约215亿美元），预计2027年2月完工，随后在第二季度进行设备安装；2）清州的P&T7先进封装工厂，投资19万亿韩元（约129亿美元），专用于HBM（高带宽存储器）的组装与测试，已于4月破土动工，预计2027年底建成；3）采购多台EUV（极紫外）光刻机——SK海力士已于3月向ASML下达了创纪录的79亿美元订单，涵盖2027年前交付的约30台扫描仪。EUV光刻技术是制造先进DRAM和HBM的核心环节，能够实现更精细的电路图案，提升芯片性能和能效。

**市场地位与性能参数：** SK海力士目前占据全球HBM市场约57%的份额，以及全球DRAM市场32%的份额。董事长崔泰源多次表示，AI（人工智能）驱动的需求将使存储器供应紧张持续到2030年。不过，上述投资项目投产时间均在2027年左右，无法缓解当前因HBM产能挤占而持续推高的DRAM价格。事实上，由于三大存储器制造商将晶圆产能转向HBM（每GB HBM消耗的硅片面积约为标准DDR5的三倍），DRAM合约价格预计将上涨至2026年。此外，SK海力士两天前刚刚超越三星电子，成为韩国市值最高的上市公司，结束了三星长达26年的榜首地位，其韩国上市股票在公告后盘后交易中上涨5.5%。

**技术影响与应用场景：** 此次大规模融资与产能扩张直接回应了AI大模型训练和推理对高带宽、低功耗存储器的爆发式需求。HBM作为GPU（图形处理器）的关键配套芯片，是AI服务器中的核心部件。SK海力士承诺在五年内将存储器晶圆产能翻倍，但承认这一时间表对缓解短期供需缺口作用有限。ADR发行的具体定价将通过7月10日前的簿记建档确定，每10份ADR代表1股普通股，总募资金额可能根据最终结果调整。美银证券、花旗、高盛和摩根大通担任承销商，监管审查预计于7月3日结束，新股将于7月29日在韩国交易所登记上市。

history.title: AI data center boom hits a human bottleneck — critical skilled labor shortages could slow deployment despite billions in funding
history.description: Data center construction is facing many challenges, and among them is a shortage of skilled hands to assemble the things.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The AI boom has already been responsible for soaring demand and subsequent shortages in the areas of GPUs, computer memory, storage (both spinning and solid-state), electrical power, water, and networking equipment. The latest bottleneck may be the people needed to build the data centers themselves.Asked by Bloomberg TV whether demand for data center construction was slowing, construction industry CEO Benoit Bazin said activity remains strong, but then identified labor as one of the industry's key bottlenecks. The executive, whose company Saint-Gobain supplies construction materials and building products used in hundreds of such projects, argued that labor shortages are already affecting projects in North America and are beginning to emerge in Europe as well.Bazin mentioned the issue only in passing during his Bloomberg appearance, but his comments point toward what is becoming an increasingly important challenge for the AI infrastructure boom. The global race to build new computing infrastructure has hyperscalers like Amazon, Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Oracle collectively committing hundreds of billions of dollars toward new facilities, but constructing a modern AI data center requires far more than just money.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: AI's demand for power is so great that the Three Mile Island nuclear plant, decomissioned in 2019, is set to reopen to serve Microsoft exclusively. (Image credit: Getty / Bloomberg)As we've reported many times before, power availability is the primary constraint facing new projects. Electrical substations, transformers, transmission infrastructure, utility connections, and even generation capacity itself are already struggling to keep pace with demand. However, a growing number of executives and analysts now argue, like Bazin, that skilled labor may be emerging as a significant secondary bottleneck.You see, unlike conventional commercial construction projects, data centers require large numbers of specialized workers. You can't simply rely on standard commercial construction crews for this stuff; you need highly specialized tradesmen, like electricians, high-voltage technicians, fiber-optic installers, HVAC specialists, controls engineers, and commissioning teams, among many others. Huge swaths of these jobs require years of training and experience, making it difficult for the labor pool to expand as quickly as AI investment has ballooned. You may like Exploding number of AI data center build-outs delay Texas housing projects Analytics group signals possible delays at 40% of AI data center construction sites Half of planned US data center builds have been delayed or canceled, growth limited by shortages of power infrastructure and parts from China The problem has become serious enough that some technology companies have begun funding workforce development efforts directly. Earlier this year, Meta partnered with CBRE on a training initiative intended to help expand the pipeline of workers qualified for data center construction and operations, reflecting concerns that labor shortages could eventually slow deployment schedules.The effects may already be spilling into other sectors. We recently highlighted how demand from large data center projects has increased competition for electricians in Texas, contributing to delays in some residential housing developments as contractors struggle to compete with the wages and budgets offered by hyperscaler-backed projects. While housing obviously won't be displaced entirely, that story is an example of how AI infrastructure spending is increasingly competing for the same pool of skilled tradespeople needed elsewhere in the economy.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Citizens gather at a rally to oppose the construction of new data centers in St. Paul, Minnesota. (Image credit: Getty Images / Universal Images Group)Also, labor is only one of several non-technical challenges facing new projects. Public opposition has become increasingly visible in some communities, particularly as residents raise concerns about electricity consumption, water usage, noise, and the broader impact of large-scale data center developments. Looking again to Texas, where numerous projects have been proposed or announced, opposition to new facilities has become a recurring topic of debate. Concerns that once focused primarily on industrial facilities and energy projects are increasingly being directed toward data centers as well.Demand for new facilities remains strong, and few observers actually expect overall construction activity to slow significantly in the near term, but building the infrastructure required to support the next generation of AI systems means solving a growing list of problems, from power generation and grid capacity to permitting, community opposition, and now, increasingly, workforce shortages. The industry has largely solved the problem of attracting capital. It can order more GPUs, buy more land, and sign larger power contracts. Producing thousands of experienced electricians and technicians, however, takes years. As the global data center boom continues, that shortage may prove to be one of the industry's most stubborn constraints. history.author: Zak Killian
history.titleZh: AI数据中心热潮遭遇人力瓶颈——关键技能劳动力短缺可能减缓部署，尽管有数十亿美元资金投入
history.descriptionZh:
人工智能（AI）产业的爆发式增长已导致GPU、计算机内存、存储设备（包括机械硬盘和固态硬盘）、电力、水资源以及网络设备等领域的需求飙升和相继短缺。如今，建设数据中心所需的人力资源可能成为最新的瓶颈。在接受彭博电视采访时，建筑材料巨头圣戈班（Saint-Gobain）的首席执行官伯努瓦·巴赞（Benoit Bazin）表示，尽管数据中心建设活动依然强劲，但劳动力已成为该行业的关键瓶颈之一。圣戈班为全球数百个数据中心项目提供建筑材料和产品，巴赞指出，劳动力短缺已影响北美项目，并开始在欧洲显现。巴赞的评论暗示了AI基础设施繁荣面临的一个日益严峻的挑战：全球竞相建设新的计算基础设施，亚马逊、微软、谷歌、Meta和甲骨文等超大规模云服务商已承诺投入数千亿美元，但现代化AI数据中心的建设远不止需要资金。

此前报道已多次强调，电力供应是新建项目面临的首要制约因素。变电站、变压器、输电基础设施、电网接入甚至发电能力本身已难以跟上需求。然而，越来越多的企业高管和分析师认为，技术工人正成为重要的二级瓶颈。与常规商业建筑项目不同，数据中心需要大量专业人才：不能仅靠普通建筑工人，而必须依赖高度专业化的技工，如电工、高压技术员、光纤安装工、暖通空调专家、控制工程师和调试团队等。这些岗位中的大部分需要多年培训和经验，使得劳动力储备难以像AI投资那样快速扩张。问题已严重到一些科技公司直接资助劳动力发展项目——今年早些时候，Meta与世邦魏理仕（CBRE）合作推出培训计划，旨在扩大合格的数据中心建设和运营工人队伍，反映出对劳动力短缺可能拖慢部署进度的担忧。

影响已开始外溢到其他经济领域。例如，德州大型数据中心项目对电工的需求加剧了竞争，导致部分住宅开发项目因承包商难以与超大规模云商支持的预算和工资竞争而延期。虽然住宅建设不会被完全挤占，但这表明AI基础设施投入正与经济的其他部分争夺同一批熟练技工。此外，劳动力只是新项目面临的非技术挑战之一。公众反对日益明显，居民对电力消耗、用水、噪音以及大型数据中心开发的影响表示担忧。同样在德州，多个提议或宣布的项目引发了反复的辩论，过去主要针对工业设施和能源项目的反对声，如今也指向数据中心。

对新设施的需求依然强劲，很少有观察者预期短期内的整体建设活动会显著放缓。但支撑下一代AI系统所需的基础设施建设意味着要解决越来越多的问题：从发电和电网容量、许可审批、社区反对，到现在日益凸显的劳动力短缺。行业已基本解决了吸引资本的问题——可以订购更多GPU、购买更多土地、签订更大规模的电力合同——但培养数千名经验丰富的电工和技术人员需要数年时间。随着全球数据中心建设热潮持续，劳动力短缺可能成为该行业最顽固的约束之一。
