history.author: Ben Stockton
history.titleZh: 立即以214美元入手32GB海盗船DDR5内存，得益于Newegg捆绑套装——AMD与Intel游戏PC组装套件大幅低于350美元市场价
history.descriptionZh:
Newegg在Prime Day期间推出了两套极具性价比的AMD和Intel平台内存套装优惠，让玩家能以远低于市场价的价格获得32GB DDR5-6000高速内存。这两套组合并非简单的单品打包，而是通过巧妙的产品搭配，将内存的实际成本压缩至约215美元（约合人民币1500元）左右，相比单独购买同规格内存节省了至少135美元。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
DDR5内存相比上一代DDR4，核心改进在于更高的数据传输速率和更低的电压。此次套装中的海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000内存（CL时序未明确但属于主流水平）运行在6000 MT/s（兆传输/秒）频率下，是当前AM5平台游戏玩家的甜点频率。它采用双通道配置（2×16GB），双通道技术允许CPU同时访问两根内存条，使内存带宽翻倍，从而显著提升游戏帧率和游戏加载速度。海盗船复仇者系列还拥有十区域可编程RGB灯效，支持个性化光效设置。

### 性能参数与对比数据
**AMD套装**：技嘉X870 Aorus Elite Wi-Fi 7冰霜白主板（价值约225美元）搭配海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000 32GB（2×16GB）内存，总价479.99美元，折算内存仅215美元。主板基于AM5插槽，支持最新AMD锐龙处理器（如9800X3D），提供12个后置USB接口（含两个40Gbps USB-C）、四个M.2固态硬盘插槽（其中三个支持PCIe 5.0 x4，一个支持PCIe 4.0），配备2.5G有线网卡和Wi-Fi 7无线网卡，最大可安装256GB DDR5内存（频率可达8200 MT/s）。主板通体白色，适合搭建全白主题主机。

**Intel套装**：技嘉Z890 Aorus Master Ultra Core主板（支持LGA 1851插槽，适配最新Arrow Lake架构酷睿Ultra 270K Plus等处理器）搭配同规格黑色版海盗船复仇者DDR5-6000 32GB内存，总价563.98美元，折算内存仅214美元（比AMD版便宜1美元）。主板提供14个后置USB接口、5个M.2插槽（其中两个支持PCIe 5.0），配备10G超高速有线网卡、Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4，支持技嘉EZ DIY易用设计辅助装机。

**价格对比**：AMD套装整体节省28%（节省184.99美元），Intel套装整体节省26%（节省200美元）。若单独购买同规格内存，市面上最低价格约为350美元，而此次套装折合内存价格仅约214-215美元，性价比突出。

### 技术影响与应用场景
这两套组合精准覆盖了当前游戏PC的两大主流平台。AMD AM5平台凭借3D V-Cache技术（如9800X3D）在游戏性能上表现卓越，而Intel LGA 1851平台则凭借高频率和多核心优势在创意工作负载中依然强势。DDR5-6000频率恰好适配AMD Zen 4/5架构的FCLK同步机制，能实现最低延迟和最佳游戏帧率。对于追求极致性价比的游戏玩家、想要升级旧平台（DDR4）的用户，以及搭建全白个性化主机的爱好者来说，这些套装是当前Prime Day期间最值得入手的硬件组合。不过优惠随时可能结束，建议有需求的用户尽快下单。

history.title: Meta pauses mandatory AI training program that tracked employee keystrokes after internal data leak exposed sensitive staff information company-wide — employees express frustration over poor handling of data
history.description: Meta has paused an internal AI training program after employee conversations, keystrokes, transcripts, and performance-related data were reportedly exposed across the company.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Meta has suspended an internal AI training program after an internal data leak exposed sensitive employee information company-wide, according to a Business Insider report on June 22. The program, introduced in April, was designed to help Meta train AI systems on real employee workflows by gathering data, but has now triggered internal backlash over privacy and data security.Screenshots obtained by Business Insider showed that data collected through the program was more broadly accessible within Meta than intended. The exposed information reportedly included private employee conversations, performance-related data, transcriptions, and activity records. Meta classified the incident as a SEV 2, on an internal scale of 0 to 5, where SEV 0 is the most severe.A Meta spokesperson confirmed that the company has paused the program while it investigates the incident. "We have carefully designed this program with privacy safeguards, and while we have no indication at this time that any data was improperly accessed by Meta employees, we're pausing it while we investigate," the spokesperson told Business Insider. The incident does not appear to be an external hack but rather an internal data mismanagement.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Meta introduced the program, called the Model Capability Initiative, to monitor employee behavior for use in improving its AI models. The program, which the company reportedly made mandatory for most staff, collected data on employees’ work activities, including keystrokes, mouse movements, conversations, transcripts, and performance-related information.Employees were reportedly uncomfortable with the idea of their keystrokes and mouse movements being recorded for AI training. Now they're finding out the data may not have been properly protected, and was widely accessible across the company rather than restricted to intended viewers. You may like After spat with Chinese gov't, Meta cuts AI Manus off from its internal systems and is 'sunsetting' platform, report claims Amazon employees admit to using AI unnecessarily to pump up internal usage scores Mark Zuckerberg says Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs to pay for AI infrastructure Screenshots reviewed by Business Insider reportedly showed employees criticizing the failure to lock down the data from the start. “I am incensed,” one employee wrote in an internal group, according to the report. Another said there was no evidence of malicious access, but called the lack of promised restrictions “super frustrating.”The episode is the latest in a frustrating stretch for Meta's workforce. The company has cut thousands of jobs in part to fund AI infrastructure behind more powerful AI systems; the same class of systems that Meta and other companies are deploying to replace workers. Building these models also requires vast amounts of training data, and Meta turned to its own employees to supply it, a move most employees were reportedly against. Now these employees have learned that the data they were compelled to hand over was not adequately secured, leaving it exposed to much of the company.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: Meta暂停强制AI培训项目：该项目曾追踪员工键盘输入，因内部数据泄露导致全公司敏感员工信息曝光——员工对糟糕的数据处理方式表示不满
history.descriptionZh:
Meta公司近日因内部数据泄露事件，被迫暂停了一项名为“模型能力倡议”（Model Capability Initiative）的内部AI训练项目。据Business Insider 6月22日报道，该项目自今年4月启动，旨在通过收集员工真实工作流程数据来训练AI系统，但如今却因隐私和数据安全问题引发内部强烈反弹。

该项目设计的初衷是让Meta的AI模型能够从员工日常工作中学习，以提升其智能化水平。具体而言，系统会记录员工的键盘敲击、鼠标移动、对话内容、会议转录以及绩效相关活动数据。Meta声称项目配备了隐私保护措施，但内部泄露的截图显示，这些本应限制在特定观察者范围内的数据，实际上在Meta公司内部被广泛访问，暴露了包括私人对话、绩效数据、转录记录等敏感信息。Meta内部将此次事件定性为SEV 2级（严重程度从0到5，0为最严重），表明问题已相当严重。

Meta发言人向Business Insider证实已暂停该项目，并正在调查。发言人表示：“我们精心设计了隐私保护措施，目前没有证据表明Meta员工不当访问了数据，但为了安全起见，我们暂停项目。”值得注意的是，这并非外部黑客攻击，而是内部数据管理失误。此前，Meta曾要求大多数员工强制参与该项目，许多员工本已对记录自己的键盘和鼠标活动感到不安，如今得知数据未得到妥善保护，愤怒情绪迅速蔓延。内部群组中，有员工直言“我气疯了”，也有人指出没有发现恶意访问，但缺乏承诺中的访问限制“令人极其沮丧”。

这一事件对Meta员工而言是雪上加霜。近年来Meta已多次大规模裁员，部分原因正是为了筹集资金建设更强大的AI基础设施——而这类AI系统正被公司部署来替代人类工作。构建这些模型需要海量训练数据，Meta转向自己员工“供应”数据，多数员工本就反对，如今又发现被迫上交的数据竟然安全漏洞百出，几乎向全公司开放。

从技术影响看，该事件暴露了AI训练数据采集中的核心伦理困境：当企业将员工行为数据作为训练素材时，如何在模型性能提升与个人隐私保护之间取得平衡？Meta的“模型能力倡议”本质上是一种“监控式学习”，试图从人类工作流程中提炼模式，但若数据访问权限设计不当，极易引发信任危机。应用场景上，这类内部数据训练法原本可能推广至其他企业AI项目，但此次泄露无疑会促使行业重新审视数据治理的边界。

目前Meta尚未公布后续改进措施，但这一事件已为整个科技行业敲响警钟：AI的进化不能以牺牲内部数据安全为代价，否则再强大的模型也将失去员工的信任基石。

history.title: NVIDIA and AWS Collaborate to Bring AI to Production at Scale
history.description: Building AI systems at scale is demanding, requiring low-latency inference, fast vector search, strong GPU price-performance and infrastructure that can grow without multiplying operational complexity.  NVIDIA’s latest work with Amazon Web Services (AWS) addresses each of those constraints. Across Amazon OpenSearch and Amazon EC2, NVIDIA AI infrastructure is giving enterprises more practical paths to deploy […]
history.content: Building AI systems at scale is demanding, requiring low-latency inference, fast vector search, strong GPU price-performance and infrastructure that can grow without multiplying operational complexity. NVIDIA’s latest work with Amazon Web Services (AWS) addresses each of those constraints. Across Amazon OpenSearch and Amazon EC2, NVIDIA AI infrastructure is giving enterprises more practical paths to deploy AI at production scale. EC2 G7 instances powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs expand the compute layer for AI, graphics, video and data analytics workloads, while the NVIDIA cuVS library accelerates the retrieval layer by making GPU-powered vector indexing the default in OpenSearch Serverless. And with AWS achieving NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud status for NVIDIA GB300, customers can trust they’re receiving peak optimized performance for their training workloads. NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition Multi-Workload GPUs Power New Amazon EC2 G7 Instances Amazon EC2 G7 instances bring NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs to AWS for AI inference, graphics, spatial computing and GPU-accelerated data analytics — delivering a new instance type engineered for production workloads that need performance without the operational overhead of a customer-managed GPU platform. Compared with G6 instances, G7 delivers up to 4.6x AI inference performance, up to 2.1x graphics performance and significantly faster GPU-accelerated data analytics on Amazon EMR using the NVIDIA cuDF library for Apache Spark workloads. With support for up to eight GPUs, 256GB of total GPU memory, 700 Gbps of EFA-enabled networking and up to 7.6TB of local NVMe SSD storage — across one-, two-, four- and eight- GPU configurations plus bare metal, coming soon — G7 instances let customers right-size infrastructure for their workloads instead of over-provisioning for them. The platform’s versatility means AI teams get lower-latency inference. Media and entertainment teams get high-resolution video workflows and rendering. Simulation, computer-aided design, virtual desktop infrastructure, gaming and spatial computing teams get the same instance type for graphics-intensive applications. And data teams can apply the GPU memory, local storage and networking improvements to analytics pipelines and vector database workloads. G7 instances are accessible through AWS Deep Learning Amazon Machine Images (AMIs), Amazon Deep Learning Containers, Amazon EMR, Amazon EKS, Amazon ECS and graphics AMIs — and coming soon to Amazon SageMaker AI. NVIDIA cuVS Makes GPU-Accelerated Vector Search the Default in Amazon OpenSearch The next generation of Amazon OpenSearch Serverless powers agentic AI and dynamic workloads with no infrastructure management required. It uses GPU-accelerated vector indexing, powered by NVIDIA cuVS, as the default compute choice for all vector collections. For teams building retrieval-augmented generation, semantic search, recommendation systems and agentic AI applications, that shift matters. It turns GPU-powered vector search from a specialized optimization project into a standard AWS capability. The customer impact is direct: vector indexing up to 10x faster at a quarter of the cost, compared with CPU-only builds — making billion-scale vector databases practical to build in under an hour. By making NVIDIA cuVS the default in OpenSearch Serverless, AWS customers get a much faster path from raw data to production-ready AI retrieval infrastructure — with serverless scaling that reduces operational overhead when workloads are idle. AWS Achieves NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud Status for GB300 Training Performance AWS has achieved NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud status on NVIDIA GB300 for training workloads. This means AWS meets the rigorous performance thresholds that NVIDIA uses to benchmark AI workloads against its reference architecture. This achievement is the result of deep co-engineering efforts between AWS and NVIDIA teams. Through the NVIDIA Exemplar Clouds initiative, developers and AI leaders can be confident they’re using consistent, high-performance cloud infrastructure for large-scale training, helping teams evaluate cloud providers with greater confidence, improve total cost of ownership and move AI projects from planning to production more efficiently. Together, these advancements reinforce every layer of the AI infrastructure stack on AWS. The throughline is the same: production-grade AI infrastructure that performs at scale, without adding operational burden to the teams running it. Learn more in this AWS blog.
history.author: Josiah Byers
history.titleZh: NVIDIA与AWS合作将AI大规模投入生产
history.descriptionZh:
随着企业级AI部署从实验阶段走向规模化生产，基础设施的延迟、成本、可扩展性和运维复杂度成为关键瓶颈。NVIDIA与Amazon Web Services（AWS）的最新合作，正是针对这些痛点，从计算层、检索层到训练层，系统性优化AI基础设施。此次发布涵盖三大核心更新：基于NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU的新Amazon EC2 G7实例、将GPU加速向量搜索默认集成到Amazon OpenSearch Serverless的NVIDIA cuVS库，以及AWS凭借NVIDIA GB300获得NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证。这三项进展共同为企业在AWS上构建生产级AI提供了更高效、更低成本、更少运维负担的路径。

**NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU驱动新Amazon EC2 G7实例**

Amazon EC2 G7实例是专门为需要高性能但不愿承担自管理GPU平台运维负担的工作负载而设计的新实例类型。它搭载NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell服务器版GPU，支持AI推理、图形处理、空间计算和GPU加速数据分析。与上一代G6实例相比，G7在AI推理性能上提升高达4.6倍，图形性能提升高达2.1倍。在Apache Spark数据分析场景下，借助NVIDIA cuDF库，在Amazon EMR上实现显著更快的GPU加速数据处理。

G7实例支持1、2、4、8GPU配置及即将推出的裸金属选项，单实例最多可配备8个GPU，总计256GB GPU显存，700 Gbps的EFA（弹性光纤适配器）网络带宽，以及高达7.6TB的本地NVMe SSD存储。这种灵活的配置使得企业可以根据工作负载精确选择计算资源，避免过度预配带来的成本浪费。

该平台的多工作负载通用性意味着不同团队可以在同一实例类型上获得针对性优化：AI团队获得更低延迟的推理；媒体和娱乐团队能处理高分辨率视频工作流及渲染；仿真、CAD、虚拟桌面基础设施、游戏和空间计算团队可运行图形密集型应用；数据团队则能利用GPU显存、本地存储和网络改进，加速分析管线和向量数据库工作负载。

在生态集成方面，G7实例可通过AWS Deep Learning AMIs、Amazon Deep Learning Containers、Amazon EMR、Amazon EKS、Amazon ECS及图形AMIs访问，并即将在Amazon SageMaker AI中提供。这种广泛的平台支持大大降低了企业的迁移和部署门槛。

**NVIDIA cuVS使GPU加速向量搜索成为Amazon OpenSearch Serverless的默认选项**

新一代Amazon OpenSearch Serverless旨在支持代理式AI和动态工作负载，而无需用户管理任何基础设施。其关键变化是：在所有向量集合中，将基于NVIDIA cuVS库的GPU加速向量索引设为默认计算选项。这意味着对于构建检索增强生成（RAG）、语义搜索、推荐系统和代理式AI应用的团队来说，GPU加速向量搜索不再是需要专门优化的项目，而是AWS的标准功能。

用户直接受益的数据是：与纯CPU构建相比，向量索引速度提升高达10倍，而成本仅为四分之一。这使得在不到一小时内构建十亿级规模的向量数据库成为现实。通过将NVIDIA cuVS设为OpenSearch Serverless的默认引擎，AWS客户可以从原始数据更快地走向生产就绪的AI检索基础设施，同时服务器无服务器（Serverless）的自动伸缩特性还能在工作负载空闲时显著降低运维成本。

**AWS凭借NVIDIA GB300训练性能获得NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证**

AWS在NVIDIA GB300训练工作负载上达到了NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud认证标准。这意味着AWS的云基础设施通过了NVIDIA设定的严格性能门槛，该门槛用于衡量AI训练工作负载是否达到NVIDIA参考架构的水平。这一成果源自AWS与NVIDIA团队的深度协同工程（co-engineering）努力。

通过NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud计划，开发者和AI领导者可以确信他们使用的是与NVIDIA参考架构一致的高性能云基础设施来进行大规模训练。这有助于团队在评估不同云提供商时更有信心，优化总体拥有成本（TCO），并更高效地将AI项目从规划阶段推进到生产阶段。

综合来看，这三项进展强化了AWS上AI基础设施的每一层。核心思路一以贯之：提供能够大规模稳定运行的生产级AI基础设施，同时不给运维团队增加额外负担。企业可以在AWS上获得更低延迟的推理、更快更便宜的向量检索、以及经过认证的高效训练环境，从而加速AI应用的部署与迭代。

history.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
history.description: Oracle is spending billions on data center infrastructure to support AI.
history.content: The growing use of AI contributed to Oracle laying off 21,000 workers in a year, according to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Monday. In its annual regulatory filing for the fiscal year ending May 31, Oracle said it has 141,000 full-time employees. In its 2025 filing, Oracle said it had 162,000 employees. The reported 12.9 percent reduction followed March reports of mass layoffs at the database management software company. “[T]he adoption and deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce,” the filing reads. However, the job cuts are also tied to large capital expenditure to build Oracle’s data center infrastructure to support AI workloads. “The majority of the initiatives undertaken by the 2026 Restructuring Plan were effected to implement our continued emphasis in developing, marketing, selling, and delivering our cloud-based offerings,” this week’s filing reads. Oracle plans to raise $45 billion to $50 billion in 2026 to expand its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure for customers like OpenAI, xAI, AMD, Nvidia, and Meta, it said in February. About half of that funding will come through debt, with the remainder coming from equity. When Oracle announced this, investors had already been concerned about Oracle’s growing debt to fuel its AI efforts. Overall, Oracle has over $120 billion in debt, per its fiscal year 2026 earnings report. In February, bondholders sued Oracle, claiming that they lost money because Oracle hid the need to raise its debt to build its AI infrastructure, Reuters reported. Investors have also been concerned about Oracle’s reliance on OpenAI, a customer that is not yet profitable and is reportedly losing billions of dollars a year. Analysts noted that Oracle’s workforce reduction will help the company’s cash flow. In March, Barclays said that Oracle makes less profit per employee than its rivals, CNBC reported at the time. In its SEC filing, Oracle said it spent $1.8 billion on restructuring costs in its fiscal year, which is a 481 percent increase from the prior fiscal year’s $374 million. Oracle also noted the drawbacks frequently associated with mass layoffs, including the potential for “reduced productivity” and “shortages of sufficiently skilled employees in certain roles, loss of valuable institutional knowledge, and damage to employee morale and retention.” “As our cloud and AI businesses grow, we will continually balance our resources and restructure our development group to help ensure we have the right people delivering the best cloud and AI products to our customers around the world,” Oracle said in a statement to CNBC. While generative AI has reignited concerns about AI taking over jobs, Oracle demonstrates one way AI can contribute to job losses beyond direct human replacements. That said, AI is increasingly common for companies to cite when letting workers go. “AI is now the leading reason companies give for cutting jobs, and the primary industry citing it is technology,” Andy Challenger, CRO at outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas, said in a May 2026 report released in June. “Technology, already the year’s biggest job cutter, saw its steepest cuts since early 2023, even as it remains the sector with the most hiring plans this year.” The outplacement firm reported in January that AI had been cited for 71,825 “job cut announcements” from 2023 to 2025.
history.author: Scharon Harding
history.titleZh: 甲骨文裁员21000人助力其债务驱动的人工智能投资
history.descriptionZh:
根据美国证券交易委员会（SEC）周一提交的文件，甲骨文（Oracle）在过去一年中因AI的广泛应用裁员2.1万人。在截至5月31日的2026财年年度监管文件中，甲骨文称其全职员工总数为14.1万人，而2025财年该数字为16.2万人，同比下降12.9%。这一裁员比例紧随3月份关于该数据库管理软件公司大规模裁员的报道。文件明确指出：“AI技术在我们运营中的采用和部署已经导致，并可能继续导致员工数量的减少。”然而，裁员也与甲骨文为支持AI工作负载而建设数据中心基础设施所需的大量资本支出密切相关。本周提交的文件称：“2026年重组计划所采取的大部分举措，都是为了落实我们对开发、营销、销售和交付云服务产品的持续重点。”

甲骨文在2月表示，计划在2026年筹集450亿至500亿美元，以扩展其Oracle Cloud Infrastructure，服务于OpenAI、xAI、AMD、Nvidia和Meta等客户。其中约一半资金将通过债务筹集，其余来自股权。当甲骨文宣布这一计划时，投资者早已对其为AI努力而不断增加债务感到担忧。根据2026财年财报，甲骨文总债务超过1200亿美元。据路透社报道，今年2月，债券持有人起诉甲骨文，声称因甲骨文隐瞒了为AI基础设施提高债务的必要性而遭受损失。投资者还担心甲骨文对尚未盈利且据报道每年亏损数十亿美元的客户OpenAI的依赖。

分析师指出，裁员将有助于改善甲骨文的现金流。今年3月，CNBC报道称巴克莱银行表示甲骨文每位员工的利润低于竞争对手。在SEC文件中，甲骨文披露本财年重组支出达18亿美元，较上一财年的3.74亿美元增长481%。甲骨文还指出了大规模裁员常伴随的弊端，包括“生产率下降”、“某些岗位严重缺乏足够技能的员工、宝贵机构知识的流失，以及员工士气和留存率受损”。甲骨文在给CNBC的声明中表示：“随着我们的云和AI业务增长，我们将持续平衡资源并重组开发团队，以确保拥有合适的人才，为全球客户提供最佳的云和AI产品。”

虽然生成式AI重新引发了AI取代工作的担忧，但甲骨文展示了一种AI导致失业的途径——并非直接替代人类，而是通过推动公司重组和资源再分配。与此同时，越来越多的公司在解雇员工时引用AI作为理由。职业转型公司Challenger, Gray & Christmas在2026年5月的报告（6月发布）中指出：“AI现在已成为公司裁员的首要原因，而引用最多的行业是科技行业。”首席执行官Andy Challenger表示：“科技行业已经是今年裁员最多的行业，其裁员幅度自2023年初以来最为剧烈，尽管该行业仍是今年招聘计划最多的领域。”该职业转型公司在1月报告称，2023年至2025年期间，AI已被引用为71,825次“裁员公告”的原因。
